Prefecture|other Makata shrines|Makata Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{出典の明記|date=2016-12-18}} {{神社 |名称=Makata Shrine |画像=[[ファイル:Makatajinjya-hondou.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|46|15.0|N|140|16|35.0|E}}}} |所在地={{jalink|Chiba Prefecture|Q80011}}{{jalink|Narita|Q273798}} Daikata 1
834 Funagata, Narita, Chiba Prefecture (Okumiya) |緯度度=35|緯度分=46|緯度秒=15.0|N(北緯)及びS(南緯)=N |経度度=140|経度分=16|経度秒=35.0|E(東経)及びW(西経)=E |祭神={{jalink|Wakumusubi||Wakumusubi-no-Kami}}
{{jalink|Wakahirume-no-Mikoto|Q11596349|Wakahirume-no-Mikoto}} (Okumiya) |社格=Shikinaisha (small) / gosha |本殿=One-bay shrine |創建=42nd year of Emperor Keiko |例祭=July 31 | 地図国コード = JP |地図=Japan Chiba |ラベル位置=bottom }} '''Makata Shrine''' (まかたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Daikata, {{jalink|Narita|Q273798}}, {{jalink|Chiba Prefecture|Q80011}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} system it was a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. It is the head shrine of the "18 Makata Shrines" (Makata Juhassha) in the {{jalink|Inba District area|Q11409099}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 42nd year of {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} (AD 113), {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} visited this area while on his eastern campaign, hung a mirror on a cedar trunk, and said: "If you revere and worship this mirror as Inba-no-Kunitama-Okitsukagami, the five grains will be abundant." This is said to be the origin of the shrine. In the 20th year of {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}}, Itokori-no-Mikoto, the 8th-generation descendant of {{jalink|Kamuyatsumimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}} and the {{jalink|Kuni no miyatsuko of Inba|Q11409105|Inba no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, built a shrine hall at present-day Funagata in Narita and enshrined Wakahirume-no-Mikoto with that mirror as the {{jalink|shintai|Q327532}}. Itokori-no-Mikoto also dug up seven jewels from beneath the cedar and additionally enshrined Wakumusubi-no-Kami with them as shintai. These two deities were called the "great deities of Makata no Ma" (magatama). In the 16th year of {{jalink|Empress Suiko|Q298057}} (AD 608), Hirokushi Tekurohiko-no-Mikoto, the 8th-generation descendant of Itokori-no-Mikoto, transferred Wakumusubi-no-Kami to present-day Daikata in Narita by divine oracle, and the former shrine became the Okumiya. When it was recorded in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}, because "Makata no Ma" shared a name with one of the {{jalink|Three Sacred Treasures|Q327465}}, one character was taken and the name was changed to "Makata Shrine." Later, because the area was a hemp-producing region, the shrine name was changed to "Asakata (Makata) Shrine." ファイル:Makata-jinjya-enkei.jpg|Panoramic view ファイル:Makata-jinjya-honden.jpg|Shrine buildings ファイル:Makata-jinjya-hondenura.jpg|Rear of main hall ファイル:Makata-jinjya-kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall ファイル:Amenohituku-jinjya.jpg|Ame-no-Hitsuku Shrine behind the main hall === Okumiya === Located in Teguros, Funagata, Narita, and called Teguro-sha. It is the inner shrine of Daikata. ファイル:Makatajinjya-zenkei.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Panoramic view ファイル:Makatajinjya-syadenzenkei.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Overall view of shrine buildings ファイル:Makatajinjya-syaden.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Shrine buildings ファイル:Makatajinjya-syaden2.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Shrine buildings from the rear ファイル:Makatajinjya-goshiboku.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Sacred tree (large cedar) ファイル:Makatajinjya-itukorinomikoto-funbo.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Traditional tomb-stone chamber of Itokori-no-Mikoto (Chiba Prefecture designated cultural property) ファイル:Makatajinjya-itukorinomikoto-funbo2.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Stone excavated from the traditional tomb of Itokori-no-Mikoto === Other Makata Shrines === Shrines named "Makata Shrine" number 18 in the {{jalink|Inba District|Q677245|Inba area}}: one in Narita including Daikata, eleven in {{jalink|Sakura|Q498011}}, two in {{jalink|Shisui|Q782157}}, two in {{jalink|Tomisato|Q692857}}, and one in {{jalink|Yachiyo|Q328651}}. It is a rare shrine name nationwide, and these shrines exist only from the east side of {{jalink|Lake Inba|Q5120731}} southward. * {{jalink|Makata Shrine (Sakura, Kanaki-cho)|Q11677653}} * {{jalink|Makata Shrine (Shisui, Shisui)|Q24876715}} === Subsidiary shrines === === Main shrine precinct === ; Sessha * Amahitsuku Shrine{{efn|On {{jalink|June 10|Q2615}} {{jalink|1944|Q5268}} (Showa 19), {{jalink|Amano Okamoto|Q11473104}} recorded a "Nichigetsu Shinji" ritual at the {{jalink|shrine office}} of Makata Shrine.}} and Daigongen Shrine [[File:天日津久神社-本殿.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Amahitsuku Shrine}}]] [[File:麻賀多神社(本宮)-大権現社-本殿.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Daigongen Shrine}}]] ; Massha * Mihato Shrine - (enshrined deities) Seoritsuhime, Ibukidonushi, Hayakitsuhime, Hayasasurahime * Furumine Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine * Inba Kuni no Miyatsuko Shrine, Maki Tarojo Shrine * Sakitama Shrine, Aoma Shrine, Sarutahiko Shrine * Tenjin Shrine [[File:麻賀多神社-(本宮)-古峯神社・三峯神社-.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Furumine Shrine and Mitsumine Shrine}}]] === Okumiya === ; Sessha * Kashiwahime Shrine, Yasushiro Inari Shrine ; Massha * Takatsu Shrine, Kuriohi Shrine, Yonao Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Sugawara Shrine ; Shrine outside the precinct * Itokori Shrine - listed as "Itokori Shrine" by Jinja Honcho; the torii inscription reads "Kanahira Shrine" === Cultural properties === [[ファイル:Makatajinjya-koudunooosugi.jpg|thumb|200px|The great {{jalink|cedar}} at Makata Shrine, also called "Kozu no Ōsugi."]] The following are designated {{jalink|cultural properties|Q858308}}{{Cite web|和書|author=Narita City Board of Education Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/bunka_sports/page215300.html|title=Notice and use of cultural properties (list of cultural properties)|work=Narita City, Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-04-24}}. * Prefecturally designated {{jalink|Natural Monument|Q23790}} ** Forest of Makata Shrine - designated {{jalink|March 8|Q2395}} {{jalink|1977|Q2481}} (Showa 52). The designated area is 6,386 square meters of shrine grove land{{R|"CityBunkazai"}}. In addition to more than 20 {{jalink|cedar|Q147388}} trees, species such as {{jalink|oak|Q796117}} and {{jalink|shii|Q1065400}} grow there. The "Kozu no Ōsugi," traditionally said to be 1,200-1,300 years old, is especially a giant tree about 40 meters tall with a trunk circumference of about 8 meters{{Cite web |和書 |url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p431-063.html |title=Forest of Makata Shrine / Chiba Prefecture |website=Official Chiba Prefecture Website (www.pref.chiba.lg.jp) |publisher=Chiba Prefectural Government |date=2024-02-27 |access-date=2025-04-21}}. * City-designated {{jalink|Tangible Cultural Property|Q2901860}} ** Main hall of Makata Shrine - designated {{jalink|November 3|Q2995}} {{jalink|1971|Q2475}} (Showa 46). * City-designated {{jalink|Intangible Folk Cultural Property}} ** Makata Shrine Kagura - designated {{jalink|October 6|Q2928}} {{jalink|1975|Q2479}} (Showa 50). * City-designated {{jalink|Historic site|Q1081138}} ** Shikinaisha Makata Shrine - designated November 3, {{jalink|1969|Q2485}} (Showa 44). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{jalink|List of shrines in Chiba Prefecture|Q11406137}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:まかたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Makata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Makata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:成田市の歴史]] [[Category:成田市の建築物]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:ちば遺産100選]] [[Category:房総の魅力500選]] [[Category:need translation]]

It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} system it was a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. It is the head shrine of the "18 Makata Shrines" (Makata Juhassha) in the {{jalink|Inba District area|Q11409099}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 42nd year of {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} (AD 113), {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} visited this area while on his eastern campaign, hung a mirror on a cedar trunk, and said: "If you revere and worship this mirror as Inba-no-Kunitama-Okitsukagami, the five grains will be abundant." This is said to be the origin of the shrine. In the 20th year of {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}}, Itokori-no-Mikoto, the 8th-generation descendant of {{jalink|Kamuyatsumimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}} and the {{jalink|Kuni no miyatsuko of Inba|Q11409105|Inba no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, built a shrine hall at present-day Funagata in Narita and enshrined Wakahirume-no-Mikoto with that mirror as the {{jalink|shintai|Q327532}}. Itokori-no-Mikoto also dug up seven jewels from beneath the cedar and additionally enshrined Wakumusubi-no-Kami with them as shintai. These two deities were called the "great deities of Makata no Ma" (magatama). In the 16th year of {{jalink|Empress Suiko|Q298057}} (AD 608), Hirokushi Tekurohiko-no-Mikoto, the 8th-generation descendant of Itokori-no-Mikoto, transferred Wakumusubi-no-Kami to present-day Daikata in Narita by divine oracle, and the former shrine became the Okumiya. When it was recorded in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}, because "Makata no Ma" shared a name with one of the {{jalink|Three Sacred Treasures|Q327465}}, one character was taken and the name was changed to "Makata Shrine." Later, because the area was a hemp-producing region, the shrine name was changed to "Asakata (Makata) Shrine." ファイル:Makata-jinjya-enkei.jpg|Panoramic view ファイル:Makata-jinjya-honden.jpg|Shrine buildings ファイル:Makata-jinjya-hondenura.jpg|Rear of main hall ファイル:Makata-jinjya-kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall ファイル:Amenohituku-jinjya.jpg|Ame-no-Hitsuku Shrine behind the main hall === Okumiya === Located in Teguros, Funagata, Narita, and called Teguro-sha. It is the inner shrine of Daikata. ファイル:Makatajinjya-zenkei.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Panoramic view ファイル:Makatajinjya-syadenzenkei.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Overall view of shrine buildings ファイル:Makatajinjya-syaden.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Shrine buildings ファイル:Makatajinjya-syaden2.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Shrine buildings from the rear ファイル:Makatajinjya-goshiboku.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Sacred tree (large cedar) ファイル:Makatajinjya-itukorinomikoto-funbo.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Traditional tomb-stone chamber of Itokori-no-Mikoto (Chiba Prefecture designated cultural property) ファイル:Makatajinjya-itukorinomikoto-funbo2.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Stone excavated from the traditional tomb of Itokori-no-Mikoto === Other Makata Shrines === Shrines named "Makata Shrine" number 18 in the {{jalink|Inba District|Q677245|Inba area}}: one in Narita including Daikata, eleven in {{jalink|Sakura|Q498011}}, two in {{jalink|Shisui|Q782157}}, two in {{jalink|Tomisato|Q692857}}, and one in {{jalink|Yachiyo|Q328651}}. It is a rare shrine name nationwide, and these shrines exist only from the east side of {{jalink|Lake Inba|Q5120731}} southward. * {{jalink|Makata Shrine (Sakura, Kanaki-cho)|Q11677653}} * {{jalink|Makata Shrine (Shisui, Shisui)|Q24876715}} === Subsidiary shrines === === Main shrine precinct === ; Sessha * Amahitsuku Shrine{{efn|On {{jalink|June 10|Q2615}} {{jalink|1944|Q5268}} (Showa 19), {{jalink|Amano Okamoto|Q11473104}} recorded a "Nichigetsu Shinji" ritual at the {{jalink|shrine office}} of Makata Shrine.}} and Daigongen Shrine [[File:天日津久神社-本殿.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Amahitsuku Shrine}}]] [[File:麻賀多神社(本宮)-大権現社-本殿.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Daigongen Shrine}}]] ; Massha * Mihato Shrine - (enshrined deities) Seoritsuhime, Ibukidonushi, Hayakitsuhime, Hayasasurahime * Furumine Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine * Inba Kuni no Miyatsuko Shrine, Maki Tarojo Shrine * Sakitama Shrine, Aoma Shrine, Sarutahiko Shrine * Tenjin Shrine [[File:麻賀多神社-(本宮)-古峯神社・三峯神社-.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Furumine Shrine and Mitsumine Shrine}}]] === Okumiya === ; Sessha * Kashiwahime Shrine, Yasushiro Inari Shrine ; Massha * Takatsu Shrine, Kuriohi Shrine, Yonao Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Sugawara Shrine ; Shrine outside the precinct * Itokori Shrine - listed as "Itokori Shrine" by Jinja Honcho; the torii inscription reads "Kanahira Shrine" === Cultural properties === [[ファイル:Makatajinjya-koudunooosugi.jpg|thumb|200px|The great {{jalink|cedar}} at Makata Shrine, also called "Kozu no Ōsugi."]] The following are designated {{jalink|cultural properties|Q858308}}{{Cite web|和書|author=Narita City Board of Education Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/bunka_sports/page215300.html|title=Notice and use of cultural properties (list of cultural properties)|work=Narita City, Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-04-24}}. * Prefecturally designated {{jalink|Natural Monument|Q23790}} ** Forest of Makata Shrine - designated {{jalink|March 8|Q2395}} {{jalink|1977|Q2481}} (Showa 52). The designated area is 6,386 square meters of shrine grove land{{R|"CityBunkazai"}}. In addition to more than 20 {{jalink|cedar|Q147388}} trees, species such as {{jalink|oak|Q796117}} and {{jalink|shii|Q1065400}} grow there. The "Kozu no Ōsugi," traditionally said to be 1,200-1,300 years old, is especially a giant tree about 40 meters tall with a trunk circumference of about 8 meters{{Cite web |和書 |url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p431-063.html |title=Forest of Makata Shrine / Chiba Prefecture |website=Official Chiba Prefecture Website (www.pref.chiba.lg.jp) |publisher=Chiba Prefectural Government |date=2024-02-27 |access-date=2025-04-21}}. * City-designated {{jalink|Tangible Cultural Property|Q2901860}} ** Main hall of Makata Shrine - designated {{jalink|November 3|Q2995}} {{jalink|1971|Q2475}} (Showa 46). * City-designated {{jalink|Intangible Folk Cultural Property}} ** Makata Shrine Kagura - designated {{jalink|October 6|Q2928}} {{jalink|1975|Q2479}} (Showa 50). * City-designated {{jalink|Historic site|Q1081138}} ** Shikinaisha Makata Shrine - designated November 3, {{jalink|1969|Q2485}} (Showa 44). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{jalink|List of shrines in Chiba Prefecture|Q11406137}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:まかたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Makata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Makata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:成田市の歴史]] [[Category:成田市の建築物]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:ちば遺産100選]] [[Category:房総の魅力500選]]

Prefecture|other Makata shrines|Makata Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{出典の明記|date=2016-12-18}} {{神社 |名称=Makata Shrine |画像=[[ファイル:Makatajinjya-hondou.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|46|15.0|N|140|16|35.0|E}}}} |所在地={{jalink|Chiba Prefecture|Q80011}}{{jalink|Narita|Q273798}} Daikata 1
834 Funagata, Narita, Chiba Prefecture (Okumiya) |緯度度=35|緯度分=46|緯度秒=15.0|N(北緯)及びS(南緯)=N |経度度=140|経度分=16|経度秒=35.0|E(東経)及びW(西経)=E |祭神={{jalink|Wakumusubi||Wakumusubi-no-Kami}}
{{jalink|Wakahirume-no-Mikoto|Q11596349|Wakahirume-no-Mikoto}} (Okumiya) |社格=Shikinaisha (small) / gosha |本殿=One-bay shrine |創建=42nd year of Emperor Keiko |例祭=July 31 | 地図国コード = JP |地図=Japan Chiba |ラベル位置=bottom }} '''Makata Shrine''' (まかたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Daikata, {{jalink|Narita|Q273798}}, {{jalink|Chiba Prefecture|Q80011}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} system it was a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. It is the head shrine of the "18 Makata Shrines" (Makata Juhassha) in the {{jalink|Inba District area|Q11409099}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 42nd year of {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} (AD 113), {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} visited this area while on his eastern campaign, hung a mirror on a cedar trunk, and said: "If you revere and worship this mirror as Inba-no-Kunitama-Okitsukagami, the five grains will be abundant." This is said to be the origin of the shrine. In the 20th year of {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}}, Itokori-no-Mikoto, the 8th-generation descendant of {{jalink|Kamuyatsumimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}} and the {{jalink|Kuni no miyatsuko of Inba|Q11409105|Inba no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, built a shrine hall at present-day Funagata in Narita and enshrined Wakahirume-no-Mikoto with that mirror as the {{jalink|shintai|Q327532}}. Itokori-no-Mikoto also dug up seven jewels from beneath the cedar and additionally enshrined Wakumusubi-no-Kami with them as shintai. These two deities were called the "great deities of Makata no Ma" (magatama). In the 16th year of {{jalink|Empress Suiko|Q298057}} (AD 608), Hirokushi Tekurohiko-no-Mikoto, the 8th-generation descendant of Itokori-no-Mikoto, transferred Wakumusubi-no-Kami to present-day Daikata in Narita by divine oracle, and the former shrine became the Okumiya. When it was recorded in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}, because "Makata no Ma" shared a name with one of the {{jalink|Three Sacred Treasures|Q327465}}, one character was taken and the name was changed to "Makata Shrine." Later, because the area was a hemp-producing region, the shrine name was changed to "Asakata (Makata) Shrine." ファイル:Makata-jinjya-enkei.jpg|Panoramic view ファイル:Makata-jinjya-honden.jpg|Shrine buildings ファイル:Makata-jinjya-hondenura.jpg|Rear of main hall ファイル:Makata-jinjya-kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall ファイル:Amenohituku-jinjya.jpg|Ame-no-Hitsuku Shrine behind the main hall === Okumiya === Located in Teguros, Funagata, Narita, and called Teguro-sha. It is the inner shrine of Daikata. ファイル:Makatajinjya-zenkei.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Panoramic view ファイル:Makatajinjya-syadenzenkei.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Overall view of shrine buildings ファイル:Makatajinjya-syaden.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Shrine buildings ファイル:Makatajinjya-syaden2.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Shrine buildings from the rear ファイル:Makatajinjya-goshiboku.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Sacred tree (large cedar) ファイル:Makatajinjya-itukorinomikoto-funbo.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Traditional tomb-stone chamber of Itokori-no-Mikoto (Chiba Prefecture designated cultural property) ファイル:Makatajinjya-itukorinomikoto-funbo2.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Stone excavated from the traditional tomb of Itokori-no-Mikoto === Other Makata Shrines === Shrines named "Makata Shrine" number 18 in the {{jalink|Inba District|Q677245|Inba area}}: one in Narita including Daikata, eleven in {{jalink|Sakura|Q498011}}, two in {{jalink|Shisui|Q782157}}, two in {{jalink|Tomisato|Q692857}}, and one in {{jalink|Yachiyo|Q328651}}. It is a rare shrine name nationwide, and these shrines exist only from the east side of {{jalink|Lake Inba|Q5120731}} southward. * {{jalink|Makata Shrine (Sakura, Kanaki-cho)|Q11677653}} * {{jalink|Makata Shrine (Shisui, Shisui)|Q24876715}} === Subsidiary shrines === === Main shrine precinct === ; Sessha * Amahitsuku Shrine{{efn|On {{jalink|June 10|Q2615}} {{jalink|1944|Q5268}} (Showa 19), {{jalink|Amano Okamoto|Q11473104}} recorded a "Nichigetsu Shinji" ritual at the {{jalink|shrine office}} of Makata Shrine.}} and Daigongen Shrine [[File:天日津久神社-本殿.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Amahitsuku Shrine}}]] [[File:麻賀多神社(本宮)-大権現社-本殿.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Daigongen Shrine}}]] ; Massha * Mihato Shrine - (enshrined deities) Seoritsuhime, Ibukidonushi, Hayakitsuhime, Hayasasurahime * Furumine Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine * Inba Kuni no Miyatsuko Shrine, Maki Tarojo Shrine * Sakitama Shrine, Aoma Shrine, Sarutahiko Shrine * Tenjin Shrine [[File:麻賀多神社-(本宮)-古峯神社・三峯神社-.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Furumine Shrine and Mitsumine Shrine}}]] === Okumiya === ; Sessha * Kashiwahime Shrine, Yasushiro Inari Shrine ; Massha * Takatsu Shrine, Kuriohi Shrine, Yonao Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Sugawara Shrine ; Shrine outside the precinct * Itokori Shrine - listed as "Itokori Shrine" by Jinja Honcho; the torii inscription reads "Kanahira Shrine" === Cultural properties === [[ファイル:Makatajinjya-koudunooosugi.jpg|thumb|200px|The great {{jalink|cedar}} at Makata Shrine, also called "Kozu no Ōsugi."]] The following are designated {{jalink|cultural properties|Q858308}}{{Cite web|和書|author=Narita City Board of Education Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/bunka_sports/page215300.html|title=Notice and use of cultural properties (list of cultural properties)|work=Narita City, Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-04-24}}. * Prefecturally designated {{jalink|Natural Monument|Q23790}} ** Forest of Makata Shrine - designated {{jalink|March 8|Q2395}} {{jalink|1977|Q2481}} (Showa 52). The designated area is 6,386 square meters of shrine grove land{{R|"CityBunkazai"}}. In addition to more than 20 {{jalink|cedar|Q147388}} trees, species such as {{jalink|oak|Q796117}} and {{jalink|shii|Q1065400}} grow there. The "Kozu no Ōsugi," traditionally said to be 1,200-1,300 years old, is especially a giant tree about 40 meters tall with a trunk circumference of about 8 meters{{Cite web |和書 |url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p431-063.html |title=Forest of Makata Shrine / Chiba Prefecture |website=Official Chiba Prefecture Website (www.pref.chiba.lg.jp) |publisher=Chiba Prefectural Government |date=2024-02-27 |access-date=2025-04-21}}. * City-designated {{jalink|Tangible Cultural Property|Q2901860}} ** Main hall of Makata Shrine - designated {{jalink|November 3|Q2995}} {{jalink|1971|Q2475}} (Showa 46). * City-designated {{jalink|Intangible Folk Cultural Property}} ** Makata Shrine Kagura - designated {{jalink|October 6|Q2928}} {{jalink|1975|Q2479}} (Showa 50). * City-designated {{jalink|Historic site|Q1081138}} ** Shikinaisha Makata Shrine - designated November 3, {{jalink|1969|Q2485}} (Showa 44). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{jalink|List of shrines in Chiba Prefecture|Q11406137}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:まかたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Makata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Makata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:成田市の歴史]] [[Category:成田市の建築物]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:ちば遺産100選]] [[Category:房総の魅力500選]][[Category:qq]]

Prefecture|other Makata shrines|Makata Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{出典の明記|date=2016-12-18}} {{神社 |名称=Makata Shrine |画像=[[ファイル:Makatajinjya-hondou.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|46|15.0|N|140|16|35.0|E}}}} |所在地={{jalink|Chiba Prefecture|Q80011}}{{jalink|Narita|Q273798}} Daikata 1
834 Funagata, Narita, Chiba Prefecture (Okumiya) |緯度度=35|緯度分=46|緯度秒=15.0|N(北緯)及びS(南緯)=N |経度度=140|経度分=16|経度秒=35.0|E(東経)及びW(西経)=E |祭神={{jalink|Wakumusubi||Wakumusubi-no-Kami}}
{{jalink|Wakahirume-no-Mikoto|Q11596349|Wakahirume-no-Mikoto}} (Okumiya) |社格=Shikinaisha (small) / gosha |本殿=One-bay shrine |創建=42nd year of Emperor Keiko |例祭=July 31 | 地図国コード = JP |地図=Japan Chiba |ラベル位置=bottom }} '''Makata Shrine''' (まかたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Daikata, {{jalink|Narita|Q273798}}, {{jalink|Chiba Prefecture|Q80011}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} system it was a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. It is the head shrine of the "18 Makata Shrines" (Makata Juhassha) in the {{jalink|Inba District area|Q11409099}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 42nd year of {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} (AD 113), {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} visited this area while on his eastern campaign, hung a mirror on a cedar trunk, and said: "If you revere and worship this mirror as Inba-no-Kunitama-Okitsukagami, the five grains will be abundant." This is said to be the origin of the shrine. In the 20th year of {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}}, Itokori-no-Mikoto, the 8th-generation descendant of {{jalink|Kamuyatsumimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}} and the {{jalink|Kuni no miyatsuko of Inba|Q11409105|Inba no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, built a shrine hall at present-day Funagata in Narita and enshrined Wakahirume-no-Mikoto with that mirror as the {{jalink|shintai|Q327532}}. Itokori-no-Mikoto also dug up seven jewels from beneath the cedar and additionally enshrined Wakumusubi-no-Kami with them as shintai. These two deities were called the "great deities of Makata no Ma" (magatama). In the 16th year of {{jalink|Empress Suiko|Q298057}} (AD 608), Hirokushi Tekurohiko-no-Mikoto, the 8th-generation descendant of Itokori-no-Mikoto, transferred Wakumusubi-no-Kami to present-day Daikata in Narita by divine oracle, and the former shrine became the Okumiya. When it was recorded in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}, because "Makata no Ma" shared a name with one of the {{jalink|Three Sacred Treasures|Q327465}}, one character was taken and the name was changed to "Makata Shrine." Later, because the area was a hemp-producing region, the shrine name was changed to "Asakata (Makata) Shrine." ファイル:Makata-jinjya-enkei.jpg|Panoramic view ファイル:Makata-jinjya-honden.jpg|Shrine buildings ファイル:Makata-jinjya-hondenura.jpg|Rear of main hall ファイル:Makata-jinjya-kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall ファイル:Amenohituku-jinjya.jpg|Ame-no-Hitsuku Shrine behind the main hall === Okumiya === Located in Teguros, Funagata, Narita, and called Teguro-sha. It is the inner shrine of Daikata. ファイル:Makatajinjya-zenkei.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Panoramic view ファイル:Makatajinjya-syadenzenkei.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Overall view of shrine buildings ファイル:Makatajinjya-syaden.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Shrine buildings ファイル:Makatajinjya-syaden2.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Shrine buildings from the rear ファイル:Makatajinjya-goshiboku.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Sacred tree (large cedar) ファイル:Makatajinjya-itukorinomikoto-funbo.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Traditional tomb-stone chamber of Itokori-no-Mikoto (Chiba Prefecture designated cultural property) ファイル:Makatajinjya-itukorinomikoto-funbo2.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Stone excavated from the traditional tomb of Itokori-no-Mikoto === Other Makata Shrines === Shrines named "Makata Shrine" number 18 in the {{jalink|Inba District|Q677245|Inba area}}: one in Narita including Daikata, eleven in {{jalink|Sakura|Q498011}}, two in {{jalink|Shisui|Q782157}}, two in {{jalink|Tomisato|Q692857}}, and one in {{jalink|Yachiyo|Q328651}}. It is a rare shrine name nationwide, and these shrines exist only from the east side of {{jalink|Lake Inba|Q5120731}} southward. * {{jalink|Makata Shrine (Sakura, Kanaki-cho)|Q11677653}} * {{jalink|Makata Shrine (Shisui, Shisui)|Q24876715}} === Subsidiary shrines === === Main shrine precinct === ; Sessha * Amahitsuku Shrine{{efn|On {{jalink|June 10|Q2615}} {{jalink|1944|Q5268}} (Showa 19), {{jalink|Amano Okamoto|Q11473104}} recorded a "Nichigetsu Shinji" ritual at the {{jalink|shrine office}} of Makata Shrine.}} and Daigongen Shrine [[File:天日津久神社-本殿.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Amahitsuku Shrine}}]] [[File:麻賀多神社(本宮)-大権現社-本殿.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Daigongen Shrine}}]] ; Massha * Mihato Shrine - (enshrined deities) Seoritsuhime, Ibukidonushi, Hayakitsuhime, Hayasasurahime * Furumine Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine * Inba Kuni no Miyatsuko Shrine, Maki Tarojo Shrine * Sakitama Shrine, Aoma Shrine, Sarutahiko Shrine * Tenjin Shrine [[File:麻賀多神社-(本宮)-古峯神社・三峯神社-.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Furumine Shrine and Mitsumine Shrine}}]] === Okumiya === ; Sessha * Kashiwahime Shrine, Yasushiro Inari Shrine ; Massha * Takatsu Shrine, Kuriohi Shrine, Yonao Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Sugawara Shrine ; Shrine outside the precinct * Itokori Shrine - listed as "Itokori Shrine" by Jinja Honcho; the torii inscription reads "Kanahira Shrine" === Cultural properties === [[ファイル:Makatajinjya-koudunooosugi.jpg|thumb|200px|The great {{jalink|cedar}} at Makata Shrine, also called "Kozu no Ōsugi."]] The following are designated {{jalink|cultural properties|Q858308}}{{Cite web|和書|author=Narita City Board of Education Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/bunka_sports/page215300.html|title=Notice and use of cultural properties (list of cultural properties)|work=Narita City, Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-04-24}}. * Prefecturally designated {{jalink|Natural Monument|Q23790}} ** Forest of Makata Shrine - designated {{jalink|March 8|Q2395}} {{jalink|1977|Q2481}} (Showa 52). The designated area is 6,386 square meters of shrine grove land{{R|"CityBunkazai"}}. In addition to more than 20 {{jalink|cedar|Q147388}} trees, species such as {{jalink|oak|Q796117}} and {{jalink|shii|Q1065400}} grow there. The "Kozu no Ōsugi," traditionally said to be 1,200-1,300 years old, is especially a giant tree about 40 meters tall with a trunk circumference of about 8 meters{{Cite web |和書 |url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p431-063.html |title=Forest of Makata Shrine / Chiba Prefecture |website=Official Chiba Prefecture Website (www.pref.chiba.lg.jp) |publisher=Chiba Prefectural Government |date=2024-02-27 |access-date=2025-04-21}}. * City-designated {{jalink|Tangible Cultural Property|Q2901860}} ** Main hall of Makata Shrine - designated {{jalink|November 3|Q2995}} {{jalink|1971|Q2475}} (Showa 46). * City-designated {{jalink|Intangible Folk Cultural Property}} ** Makata Shrine Kagura - designated {{jalink|October 6|Q2928}} {{jalink|1975|Q2479}} (Showa 50). * City-designated {{jalink|Historic site|Q1081138}} ** Shikinaisha Makata Shrine - designated November 3, {{jalink|1969|Q2485}} (Showa 44). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{jalink|List of shrines in Chiba Prefecture|Q11406137}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:まかたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Makata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Makata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:成田市の歴史]] [[Category:成田市の建築物]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:ちば遺産100選]] [[Category:房総の魅力500選]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{ill|lt=Wakahirume-no-Mikoto|Q11596349|WD=Q11596349|lt=Wakahirume-no-Mikoto}} (Okumiya) |社格=Shikinaisha (small) / gosha |本殿=One-bay shrine |創建=42nd year of Emperor Keiko |例祭=July 31 | 地図国コード = JP |地図=Japan Chiba |ラベル位置=bottom }} '''Makata Shrine''' (まかたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Daikata, {{ill|lt=Narita|Q273798|WD=Q273798}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011}}. It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} system it was a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is the head shrine of the "18 Makata Shrines" (Makata Juhassha) in the {{ill|lt=Inba District area|Q11409099|WD=Q11409099}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 42nd year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} (AD 113), {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} visited this area while on his eastern campaign, hung a mirror on a cedar trunk, and said: "If you revere and worship this mirror as Inba-no-Kunitama-Okitsukagami, the five grains will be abundant." This is said to be the origin of the shrine. In the 20th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}, Itokori-no-Mikoto, the 8th-generation descendant of {{ill|lt=Kamuyatsumimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756}} and the {{ill|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko of Inba|Q11409105|WD=Q11409105|lt=Inba no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, built a shrine hall at present-day Funagata in Narita and enshrined Wakahirume-no-Mikoto with that mirror as the {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}}. Itokori-no-Mikoto also dug up seven jewels from beneath the cedar and additionally enshrined Wakumusubi-no-Kami with them as shintai. These two deities were called the "great deities of Makata no Ma" (magatama). In the 16th year of {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057}} (AD 608), Hirokushi Tekurohiko-no-Mikoto, the 8th-generation descendant of Itokori-no-Mikoto, transferred Wakumusubi-no-Kami to present-day Daikata in Narita by divine oracle, and the former shrine became the Okumiya. When it was recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, because "Makata no Ma" shared a name with one of the {{ill|lt=Three Sacred Treasures|Q327465|WD=Q327465}}, one character was taken and the name was changed to "Makata Shrine." Later, because the area was a hemp-producing region, the shrine name was changed to "Asakata (Makata) Shrine." ファイル:Makata-jinjya-enkei.jpg|Panoramic view ファイル:Makata-jinjya-honden.jpg|Shrine buildings ファイル:Makata-jinjya-hondenura.jpg|Rear of main hall ファイル:Makata-jinjya-kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall ファイル:Amenohituku-jinjya.jpg|Ame-no-Hitsuku Shrine behind the main hall === Okumiya === Located in Teguros, Funagata, Narita, and called Teguro-sha. It is the inner shrine of Daikata. ファイル:Makatajinjya-zenkei.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Panoramic view ファイル:Makatajinjya-syadenzenkei.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Overall view of shrine buildings ファイル:Makatajinjya-syaden.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Shrine buildings ファイル:Makatajinjya-syaden2.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Shrine buildings from the rear ファイル:Makatajinjya-goshiboku.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Sacred tree (large cedar) ファイル:Makatajinjya-itukorinomikoto-funbo.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Traditional tomb-stone chamber of Itokori-no-Mikoto (Chiba Prefecture designated cultural property) ファイル:Makatajinjya-itukorinomikoto-funbo2.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Stone excavated from the traditional tomb of Itokori-no-Mikoto === Other Makata Shrines === Shrines named "Makata Shrine" number 18 in the {{ill|lt=Inba District|Q677245|WD=Q677245|lt=Inba area}}: one in Narita including Daikata, eleven in {{ill|lt=Sakura|Q498011|WD=Q498011}}, two in {{ill|lt=Shisui|Q782157|WD=Q782157}}, two in {{ill|lt=Tomisato|Q692857|WD=Q692857}}, and one in {{ill|lt=Yachiyo|Q328651|WD=Q328651}}. It is a rare shrine name nationwide, and these shrines exist only from the east side of {{ill|lt=Lake Inba|Q5120731|WD=Q5120731}} southward. * {{ill|lt=Makata Shrine (Sakura, Kanaki-cho)|Q11677653|WD=Q11677653}} * {{ill|lt=Makata Shrine (Shisui, Shisui)|Q24876715|WD=Q24876715}} === Subsidiary shrines === === Main shrine precinct === ; Sessha * Amahitsuku Shrine{{efn|On {{ill|lt=June 10|Q2615|WD=Q2615}} {{ill|lt=1944|Q5268|WD=Q5268}} (Showa 19), {{ill|lt=Amano Okamoto|Q11473104|WD=Q11473104}} recorded a "Nichigetsu Shinji" ritual at the {{ill||lt=shrine office|UNKNOWN}} of Makata Shrine.}} and Daigongen Shrine [[File:天日津久神社-本殿.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Amahitsuku Shrine}}]] [[File:麻賀多神社(本宮)-大権現社-本殿.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Daigongen Shrine}}]] ; Sessha * Kashiwahime Shrine, Yasushiro Inari Shrine ; Massha * Takatsu Shrine, Kuriohi Shrine, Yonao Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Sugawara Shrine ; Shrine outside the precinct * Itokori Shrine - listed as "Itokori Shrine" by Jinja Honcho; the torii inscription reads "Kanahira Shrine" === Cultural properties === [[ファイル:Makatajinjya-koudunooosugi.jpg|thumb|200px|The great {{ill||lt=cedar|UNKNOWN}} at Makata Shrine, also called "Kozu no Ōsugi."]] The following are designated {{ill|lt=cultural properties|Q858308|WD=Q858308}}{{Cite web|和書|author=Narita City Board of Education Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/bunka_sports/page215300.html|title=Notice and use of cultural properties (list of cultural properties)|work=Narita City, Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-04-24}}. * Prefecturally designated {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}} ** Forest of Makata Shrine - designated {{ill|lt=March 8|Q2395|WD=Q2395}} {{ill|lt=1977|Q2481|WD=Q2481}} (Showa 52). The designated area is 6,386 square meters of shrine grove land{{R|"CityBunkazai"}}. In addition to more than 20 {{ill|lt=cedar|Q147388|WD=Q147388}} trees, species such as {{ill|lt=oak|Q796117|WD=Q796117}} and {{ill|lt=shii|Q1065400|WD=Q1065400}} grow there. The "Kozu no Ōsugi," traditionally said to be 1,200-1,300 years old, is especially a giant tree about 40 meters tall with a trunk circumference of about 8 meters{{Cite web |和書 |url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p431-063.html |title=Forest of Makata Shrine / Chiba Prefecture |website=Official Chiba Prefecture Website (www.pref.chiba.lg.jp) |publisher=Chiba Prefectural Government |date=2024-02-27 |access-date=2025-04-21}}. * City-designated {{ill|lt=Tangible Cultural Property|Q2901860|WD=Q2901860}} ** Main hall of Makata Shrine - designated {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995}} {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475}} (Showa 46). * City-designated {{ill||lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|UNKNOWN}} ** Makata Shrine Kagura - designated {{ill|lt=October 6|Q2928|WD=Q2928}} {{ill|lt=1975|Q2479|WD=Q2479}} (Showa 50). * City-designated {{ill|lt=Historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138}} ** Shikinaisha Makata Shrine - designated November 3, {{ill|lt=1969|Q2485|WD=Q2485}} (Showa 44). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Chiba Prefecture|Q11406137|WD=Q11406137}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:まかたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Makata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Makata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:成田市の歴史]] [[Category:成田市の建築物]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:ちば遺産100選]] [[Category:房総の魅力500選]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

the torii inscription reads "Kanahira Shrine" === Cultural properties === [[ファイル:Makatajinjya-koudunooosugi.jpg|thumb|200px|The great {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=cedar|UNKNOWN}} at Makata Shrine, also called "Kozu no Ōsugi."]] The following are designated {{ill|lt=cultural properties|Q858308|WD=Q858308}}{{Cite web|和書|author=Narita City Board of Education Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/bunka_sports/page215300.html|title=Notice and use of cultural properties (list of cultural properties)|work=Narita City, Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-04-24}}. * Prefecturally designated {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}} ** Forest of Makata Shrine - designated {{ill|lt=March 8|Q2395|WD=Q2395}} {{ill|lt=1977|Q2481|WD=Q2481}} (Showa 52). The designated area is 6,386 square meters of shrine grove land{{R|"CityBunkazai"}}. In addition to more than 20 {{ill|lt=cedar|Q147388|WD=Q147388}} trees, species such as {{ill|lt=oak|Q796117|WD=Q796117}} and {{ill|lt=shii|Q1065400|WD=Q1065400}} grow there. The "Kozu no Ōsugi," traditionally said to be 1,200-1,300 years old, is especially a giant tree about 40 meters tall with a trunk circumference of about 8 meters{{Cite web |和書 |url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p431-063.html |title=Forest of Makata Shrine / Chiba Prefecture |website=Official Chiba Prefecture Website (www.pref.chiba.lg.jp) |publisher=Chiba Prefectural Government |date=2024-02-27 |access-date=2025-04-21}}. * City-designated {{ill|lt=Tangible Cultural Property|Q2901860|WD=Q2901860}} ** Main hall of Makata Shrine - designated {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995}} {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475}} (Showa 46). * City-designated {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|UNKNOWN}} ** Makata Shrine Kagura - designated {{ill|lt=October 6|Q2928|WD=Q2928}} {{ill|lt=1975|Q2479|WD=Q2479}} (Showa 50). * City-designated {{ill|lt=Historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138}} ** Shikinaisha Makata Shrine - designated November 3, {{ill|lt=1969|Q2485|WD=Q2485}} (Showa 44). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Chiba Prefecture|Q11406137|WD=Q11406137}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:まかたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Makata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Makata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:成田市の歴史]] [[Category:成田市の建築物]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:ちば遺産100選]] [[Category:房総の魅力500選]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Prefecture|other Makata shrines|Makata Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{出典の明記|date=2016-12-18}} {{神社 |名称=Makata Shrine |画像=[[ファイル:Makatajinjya-hondou.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|46|15.0|N|140|16|35.0|E}}}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011}}{{ill|lt=Narita|Q273798|WD=Q273798}} Daikata 1
834 Funagata, Narita, Chiba Prefecture (Okumiya) |緯度度=35|緯度分=46|緯度秒=15.0|N(北緯)及びS(南緯)=N |経度度=140|経度分=16|経度秒=35.0|E(東経)及びW(西経)=E |祭神={{ill|lt=Wakumusubi||WD=|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami}}
{{ill|lt=Wakahirume-no-Mikoto|Q11596349|WD=Q11596349|lt=Wakahirume-no-Mikoto}} (Okumiya) |社格=Shikinaisha (small) / gosha |本殿=One-bay shrine |創建=42nd year of Emperor Keiko |例祭=July 31 | 地図国コード = JP |地図=Japan Chiba |ラベル位置=bottom }} '''Makata Shrine''' (まかたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Daikata, {{ill|lt=Narita|Q273798|WD=Q273798}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} system it was a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is the head shrine of the "18 Makata Shrines" (Makata Juhassha) in the {{ill|lt=Inba District area|Q11409099|WD=Q11409099}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 42nd year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} (AD 113), {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} visited this area while on his eastern campaign, hung a mirror on a cedar trunk, and said: "If you revere and worship this mirror as Inba-no-Kunitama-Okitsukagami, the five grains will be abundant." This is said to be the origin of the shrine. In the 20th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}, Itokori-no-Mikoto, the 8th-generation descendant of {{ill|lt=Kamuyatsumimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756}} and the {{ill|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko of Inba|Q11409105|WD=Q11409105|lt=Inba no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, built a shrine hall at present-day Funagata in Narita and enshrined Wakahirume-no-Mikoto with that mirror as the {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}}. Itokori-no-Mikoto also dug up seven jewels from beneath the cedar and additionally enshrined Wakumusubi-no-Kami with them as shintai. These two deities were called the "great deities of Makata no Ma" (magatama). In the 16th year of {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057}} (AD 608), Hirokushi Tekurohiko-no-Mikoto, the 8th-generation descendant of Itokori-no-Mikoto, transferred Wakumusubi-no-Kami to present-day Daikata in Narita by divine oracle, and the former shrine became the Okumiya. When it was recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, because "Makata no Ma" shared a name with one of the {{ill|lt=Three Sacred Treasures|Q327465|WD=Q327465}}, one character was taken and the name was changed to "Makata Shrine." Later, because the area was a hemp-producing region, the shrine name was changed to "Asakata (Makata) Shrine." ファイル:Makata-jinjya-enkei.jpg|Panoramic view ファイル:Makata-jinjya-honden.jpg|Shrine buildings ファイル:Makata-jinjya-hondenura.jpg|Rear of main hall ファイル:Makata-jinjya-kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall ファイル:Amenohituku-jinjya.jpg|Ame-no-Hitsuku Shrine behind the main hall === Okumiya === Located in Teguros, Funagata, Narita, and called Teguro-sha. It is the inner shrine of Daikata. ファイル:Makatajinjya-zenkei.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Panoramic view ファイル:Makatajinjya-syadenzenkei.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Overall view of shrine buildings ファイル:Makatajinjya-syaden.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Shrine buildings ファイル:Makatajinjya-syaden2.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Shrine buildings from the rear ファイル:Makatajinjya-goshiboku.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Sacred tree (large cedar) ファイル:Makatajinjya-itukorinomikoto-funbo.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Traditional tomb-stone chamber of Itokori-no-Mikoto (Chiba Prefecture designated cultural property) ファイル:Makatajinjya-itukorinomikoto-funbo2.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Stone excavated from the traditional tomb of Itokori-no-Mikoto === Other Makata Shrines === Shrines named "Makata Shrine" number 18 in the {{ill|lt=Inba District|Q677245|WD=Q677245|lt=Inba area}}: one in Narita including Daikata, eleven in {{ill|lt=Sakura|Q498011|WD=Q498011}}, two in {{ill|lt=Shisui|Q782157|WD=Q782157}}, two in {{ill|lt=Tomisato|Q692857|WD=Q692857}}, and one in {{ill|lt=Yachiyo|Q328651|WD=Q328651}}. It is a rare shrine name nationwide, and these shrines exist only from the east side of {{ill|lt=Lake Inba|Q5120731|WD=Q5120731}} southward. * {{ill|lt=Makata Shrine (Sakura, Kanaki-cho)|Q11677653|WD=Q11677653}} * {{ill|lt=Makata Shrine (Shisui, Shisui)|Q24876715|WD=Q24876715}} === Subsidiary shrines === === Main shrine precinct === ; Sessha * Amahitsuku Shrine{{efn|On {{ill|lt=June 10|Q2615|WD=Q2615}} {{ill|lt=1944|Q5268|WD=Q5268}} (Showa 19), {{ill|lt=Amano Okamoto|Q11473104|WD=Q11473104}} recorded a "Nichigetsu Shinji" ritual at the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine office|1=UNKNOWN}} of Makata Shrine.}} and Daigongen Shrine [[File:天日津久神社-本殿.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Amahitsuku Shrine}}]] [[File:麻賀多神社(本宮)-大権現社-本殿.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Daigongen Shrine}}]] ; Massha * Mihato Shrine - (enshrined deities) Seoritsuhime, Ibukidonushi, Hayakitsuhime, Hayasasurahime * Furumine Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine * Inba Kuni no Miyatsuko Shrine, Maki Tarojo Shrine * Sakitama Shrine, Aoma Shrine, Sarutahiko Shrine * Tenjin Shrine [[File:麻賀多神社-(本宮)-古峯神社・三峯神社-.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Furumine Shrine and Mitsumine Shrine}}]] === Okumiya === ; Sessha * Kashiwahime Shrine, Yasushiro Inari Shrine ; Massha * Takatsu Shrine, Kuriohi Shrine, Yonao Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Sugawara Shrine ; Shrine outside the precinct * Itokori Shrine - listed as "Itokori Shrine" by Jinja Honcho; the torii inscription reads "Kanahira Shrine" === Cultural properties === [[ファイル:Makatajinjya-koudunooosugi.jpg|thumb|200px|The great {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=cedar|1=UNKNOWN}} at Makata Shrine, also called "Kozu no Ōsugi."]] The following are designated {{ill|lt=cultural properties|Q858308|WD=Q858308}}{{Cite web|和書|author=Narita City Board of Education Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/bunka_sports/page215300.html|title=Notice and use of cultural properties (list of cultural properties)|work=Narita City, Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-04-24}}. * Prefecturally designated {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}} ** Forest of Makata Shrine - designated {{ill|lt=March 8|Q2395|WD=Q2395}} {{ill|lt=1977|Q2481|WD=Q2481}} (Showa 52). The designated area is 6,386 square meters of shrine grove land{{R|"CityBunkazai"}}. In addition to more than 20 {{ill|lt=cedar|Q147388|WD=Q147388}} trees, species such as {{ill|lt=oak|Q796117|WD=Q796117}} and {{ill|lt=shii|Q1065400|WD=Q1065400}} grow there. The "Kozu no Ōsugi," traditionally said to be 1,200-1,300 years old, is especially a giant tree about 40 meters tall with a trunk circumference of about 8 meters{{Cite web |和書 |url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p431-063.html |title=Forest of Makata Shrine / Chiba Prefecture |website=Official Chiba Prefecture Website (www.pref.chiba.lg.jp) |publisher=Chiba Prefectural Government |date=2024-02-27 |access-date=2025-04-21}}. * City-designated {{ill|lt=Tangible Cultural Property|Q2901860|WD=Q2901860}} ** Main hall of Makata Shrine - designated {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995}} {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475}} (Showa 46). * City-designated {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Makata Shrine Kagura - designated {{ill|lt=October 6|Q2928|WD=Q2928}} {{ill|lt=1975|Q2479|WD=Q2479}} (Showa 50). * City-designated {{ill|lt=Historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138}} ** Shikinaisha Makata Shrine - designated November 3, {{ill|lt=1969|Q2485|WD=Q2485}} (Showa 44). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Chiba Prefecture|Q11406137|WD=Q11406137}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:まかたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Makata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Makata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:成田市の歴史]] [[Category:成田市の建築物]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:ちば遺産100選]] [[Category:房総の魅力500選]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Prefecture|other Makata shrines|Makata Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{出典の明記|date=2016-12-18}} {{神社 |名称=Makata Shrine |画像=[[ファイル:Makatajinjya-hondou.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|46|15.0|N|140|16|35.0|E}}}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011}}{{ill|lt=Narita|Q273798|WD=Q273798}} Daikata 1
834 Funagata, Narita, Chiba Prefecture (Okumiya) |緯度度=35|緯度分=46|緯度秒=15.0|N(北緯)及びS(南緯)=N |経度度=140|経度分=16|経度秒=35.0|E(東経)及びW(西経)=E |祭神={{ill|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami}}
{{ill|lt=Wakahirume-no-Mikoto|Q11596349|WD=Q11596349|lt=Wakahirume-no-Mikoto}} (Okumiya) |社格=Shikinaisha (small) / gosha |本殿=One-bay shrine |創建=42nd year of Emperor Keiko |例祭=July 31 | 地図国コード = JP |地図=Japan Chiba |ラベル位置=bottom }} '''Makata Shrine''' (まかたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Daikata, {{ill|lt=Narita|Q273798|WD=Q273798}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} system it was a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is the head shrine of the "18 Makata Shrines" (Makata Juhassha) in the {{ill|lt=Inba District area|Q11409099|WD=Q11409099}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 42nd year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} (AD 113), {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} visited this area while on his eastern campaign, hung a mirror on a cedar trunk, and said: "If you revere and worship this mirror as Inba-no-Kunitama-Okitsukagami, the five grains will be abundant." This is said to be the origin of the shrine. In the 20th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}, Itokori-no-Mikoto, the 8th-generation descendant of {{ill|lt=Kamuyatsumimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756}} and the {{ill|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko of Inba|Q11409105|WD=Q11409105|lt=Inba no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, built a shrine hall at present-day Funagata in Narita and enshrined Wakahirume-no-Mikoto with that mirror as the {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}}. Itokori-no-Mikoto also dug up seven jewels from beneath the cedar and additionally enshrined Wakumusubi-no-Kami with them as shintai. These two deities were called the "great deities of Makata no Ma" (magatama). In the 16th year of {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057}} (AD 608), Hirokushi Tekurohiko-no-Mikoto, the 8th-generation descendant of Itokori-no-Mikoto, transferred Wakumusubi-no-Kami to present-day Daikata in Narita by divine oracle, and the former shrine became the Okumiya. When it was recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, because "Makata no Ma" shared a name with one of the {{ill|lt=Three Sacred Treasures|Q327465|WD=Q327465}}, one character was taken and the name was changed to "Makata Shrine." Later, because the area was a hemp-producing region, the shrine name was changed to "Asakata (Makata) Shrine." ファイル:Makata-jinjya-enkei.jpg|Panoramic view ファイル:Makata-jinjya-honden.jpg|Shrine buildings ファイル:Makata-jinjya-hondenura.jpg|Rear of main hall ファイル:Makata-jinjya-kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall ファイル:Amenohituku-jinjya.jpg|Ame-no-Hitsuku Shrine behind the main hall === Okumiya === Located in Teguros, Funagata, Narita, and called Teguro-sha. It is the inner shrine of Daikata. ファイル:Makatajinjya-zenkei.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Panoramic view ファイル:Makatajinjya-syadenzenkei.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Overall view of shrine buildings ファイル:Makatajinjya-syaden.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Shrine buildings ファイル:Makatajinjya-syaden2.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Shrine buildings from the rear ファイル:Makatajinjya-goshiboku.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Sacred tree (large cedar) ファイル:Makatajinjya-itukorinomikoto-funbo.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Traditional tomb-stone chamber of Itokori-no-Mikoto (Chiba Prefecture designated cultural property) ファイル:Makatajinjya-itukorinomikoto-funbo2.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Stone excavated from the traditional tomb of Itokori-no-Mikoto === Other Makata Shrines === Shrines named "Makata Shrine" number 18 in the {{ill|lt=Inba District|Q677245|WD=Q677245|lt=Inba area}}: one in Narita including Daikata, eleven in {{ill|lt=Sakura|Q498011|WD=Q498011}}, two in {{ill|lt=Shisui|Q782157|WD=Q782157}}, two in {{ill|lt=Tomisato|Q692857|WD=Q692857}}, and one in {{ill|lt=Yachiyo|Q328651|WD=Q328651}}. It is a rare shrine name nationwide, and these shrines exist only from the east side of {{ill|lt=Lake Inba|Q5120731|WD=Q5120731}} southward. * {{ill|lt=Makata Shrine (Sakura, Kanaki-cho)|Q11677653|WD=Q11677653}} * {{ill|lt=Makata Shrine (Shisui, Shisui)|Q24876715|WD=Q24876715}} === Subsidiary shrines === === Main shrine precinct === ; Sessha * Amahitsuku Shrine{{efn|On {{ill|lt=June 10|Q2615|WD=Q2615}} {{ill|lt=1944|Q5268|WD=Q5268}} (Showa 19), {{ill|lt=Amano Okamoto|Q11473104|WD=Q11473104}} recorded a "Nichigetsu Shinji" ritual at the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine office|1=UNKNOWN}} of Makata Shrine.}} and Daigongen Shrine [[File:天日津久神社-本殿.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Amahitsuku Shrine}}]] [[File:麻賀多神社(本宮)-大権現社-本殿.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Daigongen Shrine}}]] ; Massha * Mihato Shrine - (enshrined deities) Seoritsuhime, Ibukidonushi, Hayakitsuhime, Hayasasurahime * Furumine Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine * Inba Kuni no Miyatsuko Shrine, Maki Tarojo Shrine * Sakitama Shrine, Aoma Shrine, Sarutahiko Shrine * Tenjin Shrine [[File:麻賀多神社-(本宮)-古峯神社・三峯神社-.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Furumine Shrine and Mitsumine Shrine}}]] === Okumiya === ; Sessha * Kashiwahime Shrine, Yasushiro Inari Shrine ; Massha * Takatsu Shrine, Kuriohi Shrine, Yonao Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Sugawara Shrine ; Shrine outside the precinct * Itokori Shrine - listed as "Itokori Shrine" by Jinja Honcho; the torii inscription reads "Kanahira Shrine" === Cultural properties === [[ファイル:Makatajinjya-koudunooosugi.jpg|thumb|200px|The great {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=cedar|1=UNKNOWN}} at Makata Shrine, also called "Kozu no Ōsugi."]] The following are designated {{ill|lt=cultural properties|Q858308|WD=Q858308}}{{Cite web|和書|author=Narita City Board of Education Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/bunka_sports/page215300.html|title=Notice and use of cultural properties (list of cultural properties)|work=Narita City, Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-04-24}}. * Prefecturally designated {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}} ** Forest of Makata Shrine - designated {{ill|lt=March 8|Q2395|WD=Q2395}} {{ill|lt=1977|Q2481|WD=Q2481}} (Showa 52). The designated area is 6,386 square meters of shrine grove land{{R|"CityBunkazai"}}. In addition to more than 20 {{ill|lt=cedar|Q147388|WD=Q147388}} trees, species such as {{ill|lt=oak|Q796117|WD=Q796117}} and {{ill|lt=shii|Q1065400|WD=Q1065400}} grow there. The "Kozu no Ōsugi," traditionally said to be 1,200-1,300 years old, is especially a giant tree about 40 meters tall with a trunk circumference of about 8 meters{{Cite web |和書 |url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p431-063.html |title=Forest of Makata Shrine / Chiba Prefecture |website=Official Chiba Prefecture Website (www.pref.chiba.lg.jp) |publisher=Chiba Prefectural Government |date=2024-02-27 |access-date=2025-04-21}}. * City-designated {{ill|lt=Tangible Cultural Property|Q2901860|WD=Q2901860}} ** Main hall of Makata Shrine - designated {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995}} {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475}} (Showa 46). * City-designated {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Makata Shrine Kagura - designated {{ill|lt=October 6|Q2928|WD=Q2928}} {{ill|lt=1975|Q2479|WD=Q2479}} (Showa 50). * City-designated {{ill|lt=Historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138}} ** Shikinaisha Makata Shrine - designated November 3, {{ill|lt=1969|Q2485|WD=Q2485}} (Showa 44). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Chiba Prefecture|Q11406137|WD=Q11406137}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:まかたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Makata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Makata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:成田市の歴史]] [[Category:成田市の建築物]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:ちば遺産100選]] [[Category:房総の魅力500選]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It is the head shrine of the "18 Makata Shrines" (Makata Juhassha) in the {{ill|lt=Inba District area|Q11409099|WD=Q11409099}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 42nd year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} (AD 113), {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} visited this area while on his eastern campaign, hung a mirror on a cedar trunk, and said: "If you revere and worship this mirror as Inba-no-Kunitama-Okitsukagami, the five grains will be abundant." This is said to be the origin of the shrine. In the 20th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}, Itokori-no-Mikoto, the 8th-generation descendant of {{ill|lt=Kamuyatsumimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756}} and the {{ill|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko of Inba|Q11409105|WD=Q11409105|lt=Inba no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, built a shrine hall at present-day Funagata in Narita and enshrined Wakahirume-no-Mikoto with that mirror as the {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}}. Itokori-no-Mikoto also dug up seven jewels from beneath the cedar and additionally enshrined Wakumusubi-no-Kami with them as shintai. These two deities were called the "great deities of Makata no Ma" (magatama). In the 16th year of {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057}} (AD 608), Hirokushi Tekurohiko-no-Mikoto, the 8th-generation descendant of Itokori-no-Mikoto, transferred Wakumusubi-no-Kami to present-day Daikata in Narita by divine oracle, and the former shrine became the Okumiya. When it was recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, because "Makata no Ma" shared a name with one of the {{ill|lt=Three Sacred Treasures|Q327465|WD=Q327465}}, one character was taken and the name was changed to "Makata Shrine." Later, because the area was a hemp-producing region, the shrine name was changed to "Asakata (Makata) Shrine." ファイル:Makata-jinjya-enkei.jpg|Panoramic view ファイル:Makata-jinjya-honden.jpg|Shrine buildings ファイル:Makata-jinjya-hondenura.jpg|Rear of main hall ファイル:Makata-jinjya-kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall ファイル:Amenohituku-jinjya.jpg|Ame-no-Hitsuku Shrine behind the main hall === Okumiya === Located in Teguros, Funagata, Narita, and called Teguro-sha. It is the inner shrine of Daikata. ファイル:Makatajinjya-zenkei.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Panoramic view ファイル:Makatajinjya-syadenzenkei.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Overall view of shrine buildings ファイル:Makatajinjya-syaden.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Shrine buildings ファイル:Makatajinjya-syaden2.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Shrine buildings from the rear ファイル:Makatajinjya-goshiboku.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Sacred tree (large cedar) ファイル:Makatajinjya-itukorinomikoto-funbo.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Traditional tomb-stone chamber of Itokori-no-Mikoto (Chiba Prefecture designated cultural property) ファイル:Makatajinjya-itukorinomikoto-funbo2.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Stone excavated from the traditional tomb of Itokori-no-Mikoto === Other Makata Shrines === Shrines named "Makata Shrine" number 18 in the {{ill|lt=Inba District|Q677245|WD=Q677245|lt=Inba area}}: one in Narita including Daikata, eleven in {{ill|lt=Sakura|Q498011|WD=Q498011}}, two in {{ill|lt=Shisui|Q782157|WD=Q782157}}, two in {{ill|lt=Tomisato|Q692857|WD=Q692857}}, and one in {{ill|lt=Yachiyo|Q328651|WD=Q328651}}. It is a rare shrine name nationwide, and these shrines exist only from the east side of {{ill|lt=Lake Inba|Q5120731|WD=Q5120731}} southward. * {{ill|lt=Makata Shrine (Sakura, Kanaki-cho)|Q11677653|WD=Q11677653}} * {{ill|lt=Makata Shrine (Shisui, Shisui)|Q24876715|WD=Q24876715}} === Subsidiary shrines === === Main shrine precinct === ; the torii inscription reads "Kanahira Shrine" === Cultural properties === [[ファイル:Makatajinjya-koudunooosugi.jpg|thumb|200px|The great {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=cedar|1=UNKNOWN}} at Makata Shrine, also called "Kozu no Ōsugi."]] The following are designated {{ill|lt=cultural properties|Q858308|WD=Q858308}}{{Cite web|和書|author=Narita City Board of Education Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/bunka_sports/page215300.html|title=Notice and use of cultural properties (list of cultural properties)|work=Narita City, Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-04-24}}. * Prefecturally designated {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}} ** Forest of Makata Shrine - designated {{ill|lt=March 8|Q2395|WD=Q2395}} {{ill|lt=1977|Q2481|WD=Q2481}} (Showa 52). The designated area is 6,386 square meters of shrine grove land{{R|"CityBunkazai"}}. In addition to more than 20 {{ill|lt=cedar|Q147388|WD=Q147388}} trees, species such as {{ill|lt=oak|Q796117|WD=Q796117}} and {{ill|lt=shii|Q1065400|WD=Q1065400}} grow there. The "Kozu no Ōsugi," traditionally said to be 1,200-1,300 years old, is especially a giant tree about 40 meters tall with a trunk circumference of about 8 meters{{Cite web |和書 |url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p431-063.html |title=Forest of Makata Shrine / Chiba Prefecture |website=Official Chiba Prefecture Website (www.pref.chiba.lg.jp) |publisher=Chiba Prefectural Government |date=2024-02-27 |access-date=2025-04-21}}. * City-designated {{ill|lt=Tangible Cultural Property|Q2901860|WD=Q2901860}} ** Main hall of Makata Shrine - designated {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995}} {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475}} (Showa 46). * City-designated {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Makata Shrine Kagura - designated {{ill|lt=October 6|Q2928|WD=Q2928}} {{ill|lt=1975|Q2479|WD=Q2479}} (Showa 50). * City-designated {{ill|lt=Historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138}} ** Shikinaisha Makata Shrine - designated November 3, {{ill|lt=1969|Q2485|WD=Q2485}} (Showa 44). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Chiba Prefecture|Q11406137|WD=Q11406137}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:まかたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Makata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Makata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:成田市の歴史]] [[Category:成田市の建築物]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:ちば遺産100選]] [[Category:房総の魅力500選]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It is the head shrine of the "18 Makata Shrines" (Makata Juhassha) in the {{ill|lt=Inba District area|Q11409099|WD=Q11409099}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 42nd year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} (AD 113), {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} visited this area while on his eastern campaign, hung a mirror on a cedar trunk, and said: "If you revere and worship this mirror as Inba-no-Kunitama-Okitsukagami, the five grains will be abundant." This is said to be the origin of the shrine. In the 20th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}, Itokori-no-Mikoto, the 8th-generation descendant of {{ill|lt=Kamuyatsumimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756}} and the {{ill|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko of Inba|Q11409105|WD=Q11409105|lt=Inba no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, built a shrine hall at present-day Funagata in Narita and enshrined Wakahirume-no-Mikoto with that mirror as the {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}}. Itokori-no-Mikoto also dug up seven jewels from beneath the cedar and additionally enshrined Wakumusubi-no-Kami with them as shintai. These two deities were called the "great deities of Makata no Ma" (magatama). In the 16th year of {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057}} (AD 608), Hirokushi Tekurohiko-no-Mikoto, the 8th-generation descendant of Itokori-no-Mikoto, transferred Wakumusubi-no-Kami to present-day Daikata in Narita by divine oracle, and the former shrine became the Okumiya. When it was recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, because "Makata no Ma" shared a name with one of the {{ill|lt=Three Sacred Treasures|Q327465|WD=Q327465}}, one character was taken and the name was changed to "Makata Shrine." The designated area is 6,386 square meters of shrine grove land{{R|"CityBunkazai"}}. In addition to more than 20 {{ill|lt=cedar|Q147388|WD=Q147388}} trees, species such as {{ill|lt=oak|Q796117|WD=Q796117}} and {{ill|lt=shii|Q1065400|WD=Q1065400}} grow there. The "Kozu no Ōsugi," traditionally said to be 1,200-1,300 years old, is especially a giant tree about 40 meters tall with a trunk circumference of about 8 meters{{Cite web |和書 |url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p431-063.html |title=Forest of Makata Shrine / Chiba Prefecture |website=Official Chiba Prefecture Website (www.pref.chiba.lg.jp) |publisher=Chiba Prefectural Government |date=2024-02-27 |access-date=2025-04-21}}. * City-designated {{ill|lt=Tangible Cultural Property|Q2901860|WD=Q2901860}} ** Main hall of Makata Shrine - designated {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995}} {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475}} (Showa 46). * City-designated {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Makata Shrine Kagura - designated {{ill|lt=October 6|Q2928|WD=Q2928}} {{ill|lt=1975|Q2479|WD=Q2479}} (Showa 50). * City-designated {{ill|lt=Historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138}} ** Shikinaisha Makata Shrine - designated November 3, {{ill|lt=1969|Q2485|WD=Q2485}} (Showa 44). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Chiba Prefecture|Q11406137|WD=Q11406137}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:まかたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Makata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Makata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:成田市の歴史]] [[Category:成田市の建築物]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:ちば遺産100選]] [[Category:房総の魅力500選]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Prefecture|other Makata shrines|Makata Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{出典の明記|date=2016-12-18}} {{神社 |名称=Makata Shrine |画像=[[ファイル:Makatajinjya-hondou.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|46|15.0|N|140|16|35.0|E}}}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011}}{{ill|lt=Narita|Q273798|WD=Q273798}} Daikata 1
834 Funagata, Narita, Chiba Prefecture (Okumiya) |緯度度=35|緯度分=46|緯度秒=15.0|N(北緯)及びS(南緯)=N |経度度=140|経度分=16|経度秒=35.0|E(東経)及びW(西経)=E |祭神={{ill|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami}}
{{ill|lt=Wakahirume-no-Mikoto|Q11596349|WD=Q11596349|lt=Wakahirume-no-Mikoto}} (Okumiya) |社格=Shikinaisha (small) / gosha |本殿=One-bay shrine |創建=42nd year of Emperor Keiko |例祭=July 31 | 地図国コード = JP |地図=Japan Chiba |ラベル位置=bottom }} '''Makata Shrine''' (まかたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Daikata, {{ill|lt=Narita|Q273798|WD=Q273798}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} system it was a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is the head shrine of the "18 Makata Shrines" (Makata Juhassha) in the {{ill|lt=Inba District area|Q11409099|WD=Q11409099}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 42nd year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} (AD 113), {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} visited this area while on his eastern campaign, hung a mirror on a cedar trunk, and said: "If you revere and worship this mirror as Inba-no-Kunitama-Okitsukagami, the five grains will be abundant." This is said to be the origin of the shrine. In the 20th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}, Itokori-no-Mikoto, the 8th-generation descendant of {{ill|lt=Kamuyatsumimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756}} and the {{ill|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko of Inba|Q11409105|WD=Q11409105|lt=Inba no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, built a shrine hall at present-day Funagata in Narita and enshrined Wakahirume-no-Mikoto with that mirror as the {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}}. Itokori-no-Mikoto also dug up seven jewels from beneath the cedar and additionally enshrined Wakumusubi-no-Kami with them as shintai. These two deities were called the "great deities of Makata no Ma" (magatama). In the 16th year of {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057}} (AD 608), Hirokushi Tekurohiko-no-Mikoto, the 8th-generation descendant of Itokori-no-Mikoto, transferred Wakumusubi-no-Kami to present-day Daikata in Narita by divine oracle, and the former shrine became the Okumiya. When it was recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, because "Makata no Ma" shared a name with one of the {{ill|lt=Three Sacred Treasures|Q327465|WD=Q327465}}, one character was taken and the name was changed to "Makata Shrine." Later, because the area was a hemp-producing region, the shrine name was changed to "Asakata (Makata) Shrine." ファイル:Makata-jinjya-enkei.jpg|Panoramic view ファイル:Makata-jinjya-honden.jpg|Shrine buildings ファイル:Makata-jinjya-hondenura.jpg|Rear of main hall ファイル:Makata-jinjya-kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall ファイル:Amenohituku-jinjya.jpg|Ame-no-Hitsuku Shrine behind the main hall === Okumiya === Located in Teguros, Funagata, Narita, and called Teguro-sha. It is the inner shrine of Daikata. ファイル:Makatajinjya-zenkei.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Panoramic view ファイル:Makatajinjya-syadenzenkei.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Overall view of shrine buildings ファイル:Makatajinjya-syaden.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Shrine buildings ファイル:Makatajinjya-syaden2.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Shrine buildings from the rear ファイル:Makatajinjya-goshiboku.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Sacred tree (large cedar) ファイル:Makatajinjya-itukorinomikoto-funbo.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Traditional tomb-stone chamber of Itokori-no-Mikoto (Chiba Prefecture designated cultural property) ファイル:Makatajinjya-itukorinomikoto-funbo2.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Stone excavated from the traditional tomb of Itokori-no-Mikoto === Other Makata Shrines === Shrines named "Makata Shrine" number 18 in the {{ill|lt=Inba District|Q677245|WD=Q677245|lt=Inba area}}: one in Narita including Daikata, eleven in {{ill|lt=Sakura|Q498011|WD=Q498011}}, two in {{ill|lt=Shisui|Q782157|WD=Q782157}}, two in {{ill|lt=Tomisato|Q692857|WD=Q692857}}, and one in {{ill|lt=Yachiyo|Q328651|WD=Q328651}}. It is a rare shrine name nationwide, and these shrines exist only from the east side of {{ill|lt=Lake Inba|Q5120731|WD=Q5120731}} southward. * {{ill|lt=Makata Shrine (Sakura, Kanaki-cho)|Q11677653|WD=Q11677653}} * {{ill|lt=Makata Shrine (Shisui, Shisui)|Q24876715|WD=Q24876715}} === Subsidiary shrines === === Main shrine precinct === ; Sessha * Amahitsuku Shrine{{efn|On {{ill|lt=June 10|Q2615|WD=Q2615}} {{ill|lt=1944|Q5268|WD=Q5268}} (Showa 19), {{ill|lt=Amano Okamoto|Q11473104|WD=Q11473104}} recorded a "Nichigetsu Shinji" ritual at the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine office|1=UNKNOWN}} of Makata Shrine.}} and Daigongen Shrine [[File:天日津久神社-本殿.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Amahitsuku Shrine}}]] [[File:麻賀多神社(本宮)-大権現社-本殿.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Daigongen Shrine}}]] ; Massha * Mihato Shrine - (enshrined deities) Seoritsuhime, Ibukidonushi, Hayakitsuhime, Hayasasurahime * Furumine Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine * Inba Kuni no Miyatsuko Shrine, Maki Tarojo Shrine * Sakitama Shrine, Aoma Shrine, Sarutahiko Shrine * Tenjin Shrine [[File:麻賀多神社-(本宮)-古峯神社・三峯神社-.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Furumine Shrine and Mitsumine Shrine}}]] === Okumiya === ; Sessha * Kashiwahime Shrine, Yasushiro Inari Shrine ; Massha * Takatsu Shrine, Kuriohi Shrine, Yonao Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Sugawara Shrine ; Shrine outside the precinct * Itokori Shrine - listed as "Itokori Shrine" by Jinja Honcho; the torii inscription reads "Kanahira Shrine" === Cultural properties === [[ファイル:Makatajinjya-koudunooosugi.jpg|thumb|200px|The great {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=cedar|1=UNKNOWN}} at Makata Shrine, also called "Kozu no Ōsugi."]] The following are designated {{ill|lt=cultural properties|Q858308|WD=Q858308}}{{Cite web|和書|author=Narita City Board of Education Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/bunka_sports/page215300.html|title=Notice and use of cultural properties (list of cultural properties)|work=Narita City, Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-04-24}}. * Prefecturally designated {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}} ** Forest of Makata Shrine - designated {{ill|lt=March 8|Q2395|WD=Q2395}} {{ill|lt=1977|Q2481|WD=Q2481}} (Showa 52). The designated area is 6,386 square meters of shrine grove land{{R|"CityBunkazai"}}. In addition to more than 20 {{ill|lt=cedar|Q147388|WD=Q147388}} trees, species such as {{ill|lt=oak|Q796117|WD=Q796117}} and {{ill|lt=shii|Q1065400|WD=Q1065400}} grow there. The "Kozu no Ōsugi," traditionally said to be 1,200-1,300 years old, is especially a giant tree about 40 meters tall with a trunk circumference of about 8 meters{{Cite web |和書 |url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p431-063.html |title=Forest of Makata Shrine / Chiba Prefecture |website=Official Chiba Prefecture Website (www.pref.chiba.lg.jp) |publisher=Chiba Prefectural Government |date=2024-02-27 |access-date=2025-04-21}}. * City-designated {{ill|lt=Tangible Cultural Property|Q2901860|WD=Q2901860}} ** Main hall of Makata Shrine - designated {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995}} {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475}} (Showa 46). * City-designated {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Makata Shrine Kagura - designated {{ill|lt=October 6|Q2928|WD=Q2928}} {{ill|lt=1975|Q2479|WD=Q2479}} (Showa 50). * City-designated {{ill|lt=Historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138}} ** Shikinaisha Makata Shrine - designated November 3, {{ill|lt=1969|Q2485|WD=Q2485}} (Showa 44). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Chiba Prefecture|Q11406137|WD=Q11406137}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:まかたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Makata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Makata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:成田市の歴史]] [[Category:成田市の建築物]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:ちば遺産100選]] [[Category:房総の魅力500選]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]
