(disambiguation)}} {{出典の明記|date=2016-12-18}} {{神社 |名称=Makata Shrine |画像=[[ファイル:Makatajinjya-hondou.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|46|15.0|N|140|16|35.0|E}}}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Narita|Q273798|WD=Q273798|1=Narita, Chiba}} Daikata 1
834 Funagata, Narita, Chiba Prefecture (Okumiya) |緯度度=35|緯度分=46|緯度秒=15.0|N(北緯)及びS(南緯)=N |経度度=140|経度分=16|経度秒=35.0|E(東経)及びW(西経)=E |祭神={{ill|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=Wakumusubi}}
{{ill|lt=Wakahirume-no-Mikoto|Q11596349|WD=Q11596349|lt=Wakahirume-no-Mikoto|1=Wakahiru-me}} (Okumiya) |社格=Shikinaisha (small) / gosha |本殿=One-bay shrine |創建=42nd year of Emperor Keiko |例祭=July 31 | 地図国コード = JP |地図=Japan Chiba |ラベル位置=bottom }} '''Makata Shrine''' (まかたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Daikata, {{ill|lt=Narita|Q273798|WD=Q273798|1=Narita, Chiba}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is the head shrine of the "18 Makata Shrines" (Makata Juhassha) in the {{ill|lt=Inba District area|Q11409099|WD=Q11409099|1=Inba District area}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 42nd year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} (AD 113), {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} visited this area while on his eastern campaign, hung a mirror on a cedar trunk, and said: "If you revere and worship this mirror as Inba-no-Kunitama-Okitsukagami, the five grains will be abundant." This is said to be the origin of the shrine. In the 20th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, Itokori-no-Mikoto, the 8th-generation descendant of {{ill|lt=Kamuyatsumimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} and the {{ill|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko of Inba|Q11409105|WD=Q11409105|lt=Inba no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Inba no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, built a shrine hall at present-day Funagata in Narita and enshrined Wakahirume-no-Mikoto with that mirror as the {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}. Itokori-no-Mikoto also dug up seven jewels from beneath the cedar and additionally enshrined Wakumusubi-no-Kami with them as shintai. These two deities were called the "great deities of Makata no Ma" (magatama). In the 16th year of {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} (AD 608), Hirokushi Tekurohiko-no-Mikoto, the 8th-generation descendant of Itokori-no-Mikoto, transferred Wakumusubi-no-Kami to present-day Daikata in Narita by divine oracle, and the former shrine became the Okumiya. When it was recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, because "Makata no Ma" shared a name with one of the {{ill|lt=Three Sacred Treasures|Q327465|WD=Q327465|1=Imperial Regalia of Japan}}, one character was taken and the name was changed to "Makata Shrine." Later, because the area was a hemp-producing region, the shrine name was changed to "Asakata (Makata) Shrine." ファイル:Makata-jinjya-enkei.jpg|Panoramic view ファイル:Makata-jinjya-honden.jpg|Shrine buildings ファイル:Makata-jinjya-hondenura.jpg|Rear of main hall ファイル:Makata-jinjya-kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall ファイル:Amenohituku-jinjya.jpg|Ame-no-Hitsuku Shrine behind the main hall === Okumiya === Located in Teguros, Funagata, Narita, and called Teguro-sha. It is the inner shrine of Daikata. ファイル:Makatajinjya-zenkei.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Panoramic view ファイル:Makatajinjya-syadenzenkei.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Overall view of shrine buildings ファイル:Makatajinjya-syaden.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Shrine buildings ファイル:Makatajinjya-syaden2.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Shrine buildings from the rear ファイル:Makatajinjya-goshiboku.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Sacred tree (large cedar) ファイル:Makatajinjya-itukorinomikoto-funbo.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Traditional tomb-stone chamber of Itokori-no-Mikoto (Chiba Prefecture designated cultural property) ファイル:Makatajinjya-itukorinomikoto-funbo2.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Stone excavated from the traditional tomb of Itokori-no-Mikoto === Other Makata Shrines === Shrines named "Makata Shrine" number 18 in the {{ill|lt=Inba District|Q677245|WD=Q677245|lt=Inba area|1=Inba District}}: one in Narita including Daikata, eleven in {{ill|lt=Sakura|Q498011|WD=Q498011|1=Sakura, Chiba}}, two in {{ill|lt=Shisui|Q782157|WD=Q782157|1=Shisui}}, two in {{ill|lt=Tomisato|Q692857|WD=Q692857|1=Tomisato}}, and one in {{ill|lt=Yachiyo|Q328651|WD=Q328651|1=Yachiyo, Chiba}}. It is a rare shrine name nationwide, and these shrines exist only from the east side of {{ill|lt=Lake Inba|Q5120731|WD=Q5120731|1=Inbanuma}} southward. * {{ill|lt=Makata Shrine (Sakura, Kanaki-cho)|Q11677653|WD=Q11677653|1=Makata Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Makata Shrine (Shisui, Shisui)|Q24876715|WD=Q24876715|1=Makata- Shrine}} === Subsidiary shrines === === Main shrine precinct === ; Sessha * Amahitsuku Shrine{{efn|On {{ill|lt=June 10|Q2615|WD=Q2615|1=June 10}} {{ill|lt=1944|Q5268|WD=Q5268|1=1944}} (Showa 19), {{ill|lt=Amano Okamoto|Q11473104|WD=Q11473104|1=Tenmei Okamoto}} recorded a "Nichigetsu Shinji" ritual at the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine office|1=UNKNOWN}} of Makata Shrine.}} and Daigongen Shrine [[File:天日津久神社-本殿.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Amahitsuku Shrine}}]] [[File:麻賀多神社(本宮)-大権現社-本殿.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Daigongen Shrine}}]] ; Massha * Mihato Shrine - (enshrined deities) Seoritsuhime, Ibukidonushi, Hayakitsuhime, Hayasasurahime * Furumine Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine * Inba Kuni no Miyatsuko Shrine, Maki Tarojo Shrine * Sakitama Shrine, Aoma Shrine, Sarutahiko Shrine * Tenjin Shrine [[File:麻賀多神社-(本宮)-古峯神社・三峯神社-.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Furumine Shrine and Mitsumine Shrine}}]] === Okumiya === ; Sessha * Kashiwahime Shrine, Yasushiro Inari Shrine ; Massha * Takatsu Shrine, Kuriohi Shrine, Yonao Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Sugawara Shrine ; Shrine outside the precinct * Itokori Shrine - listed as "Itokori Shrine" by Jinja Honcho; the torii inscription reads "Kanahira Shrine" === Cultural properties === [[ファイル:Makatajinjya-koudunooosugi.jpg|thumb|200px|The great {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=cedar|1=UNKNOWN}} at Makata Shrine, also called "Kozu no Ōsugi."]] The following are designated {{ill|lt=cultural properties|Q858308|WD=Q858308|1=Cultural Property (Japan)}}{{Cite web|和書|author=Narita City Board of Education Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/bunka_sports/page215300.html|title=Notice and use of cultural properties (list of cultural properties)|work=Narita City, Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-04-24}}. * Prefecturally designated {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} ** Forest of Makata Shrine - designated {{ill|lt=March 8|Q2395|WD=Q2395|1=March 8}} {{ill|lt=1977|Q2481|WD=Q2481|1=1977}} (Showa 52). The designated area is 6,386 square meters of shrine grove land{{R|"CityBunkazai"}}. In addition to more than 20 {{ill|lt=cedar|Q147388|WD=Q147388|1=Cryptomeria}} trees, species such as {{ill|lt=oak|Q796117|WD=Q796117|1=Cyclobalanopsis}} and {{ill|lt=shii|Q1065400|WD=Q1065400|1=Castanopsis}} grow there. The "Kozu no Ōsugi," traditionally said to be 1,200-1,300 years old, is especially a giant tree about 40 meters tall with a trunk circumference of about 8 meters{{Cite web |和書 |url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p431-063.html |title=Forest of Makata Shrine / Chiba Prefecture |website=Official Chiba Prefecture Website (www.pref.chiba.lg.jp) |publisher=Chiba Prefectural Government |date=2024-02-27 |access-date=2025-04-21}}. * City-designated {{ill|lt=Tangible Cultural Property|Q2901860|WD=Q2901860|1=Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)}} ** Main hall of Makata Shrine - designated {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995|1=November 3}} {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475|1=1971}} (Showa 46). * City-designated {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Makata Shrine Kagura - designated {{ill|lt=October 6|Q2928|WD=Q2928|1=October 6}} {{ill|lt=1975|Q2479|WD=Q2479|1=1975}} (Showa 50). * City-designated {{ill|lt=Historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138|1=Historic site}} ** Shikinaisha Makata Shrine - designated November 3, {{ill|lt=1969|Q2485|WD=Q2485|1=1969}} (Showa 44). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Chiba Prefecture|Q11406137|WD=Q11406137|1=list of Shrines in Chiba Prefecture}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Makata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Makata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:成田市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Narita, Chiba]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Chiba Heritage Top 100]] [[Category:房総の魅力500選]][[Category:qq]]

{{commons category|Category:Makata-jinja}} [[JA:麻賀多神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11677652}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|Makata Shrine in Narita, Chiba Prefecture|other Makata shrines|Makata Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{出典の明記|date=2016-12-18}} {{神社 |名称=Makata Shrine |画像=[[ファイル:Makatajinjya-hondou.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|46|15.0|N|140|16|35.0|E}}}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Narita|Q273798|WD=Q273798|1=Narita, Chiba}} Daikata 1
834 Funagata, Narita, Chiba Prefecture (Okumiya) |緯度度=35|緯度分=46|緯度秒=15.0|N(北緯)及びS(南緯)=N |経度度=140|経度分=16|経度秒=35.0|E(東経)及びW(西経)=E |祭神={{ill|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=Wakumusubi}}
{{ill|lt=Wakahirume-no-Mikoto|Q11596349|WD=Q11596349|lt=Wakahirume-no-Mikoto|1=Wakahiru-me}} (Okumiya) |社格=Shikinaisha (small) / gosha |本殿=One-bay shrine |創建=42nd year of Emperor Keiko |例祭=July 31 | 地図国コード = JP |地図=Japan Chiba |ラベル位置=bottom }} '''Makata Shrine''' (まかたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Daikata, {{ill|lt=Narita|Q273798|WD=Q273798|1=Narita, Chiba}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is the head shrine of the "18 Makata Shrines" (Makata Juhassha) in the {{ill|lt=Inba District area|Q11409099|WD=Q11409099|1=Inba District area}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 42nd year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} (AD 113), {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} visited this area while on his eastern campaign, hung a mirror on a cedar trunk, and said: "If you revere and worship this mirror as Inba-no-Kunitama-Okitsukagami, the five grains will be abundant." This is said to be the origin of the shrine. In the 20th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, Itokori-no-Mikoto, the 8th-generation descendant of {{ill|lt=Kamuyatsumimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} and the {{ill|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko of Inba|Q11409105|WD=Q11409105|lt=Inba no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Inba no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, built a shrine hall at present-day Funagata in Narita and enshrined Wakahirume-no-Mikoto with that mirror as the {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}. Itokori-no-Mikoto also dug up seven jewels from beneath the cedar and additionally enshrined Wakumusubi-no-Kami with them as shintai. These two deities were called the "great deities of Makata no Ma" (magatama). In the 16th year of {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} (AD 608), Hirokushi Tekurohiko-no-Mikoto, the 8th-generation descendant of Itokori-no-Mikoto, transferred Wakumusubi-no-Kami to present-day Daikata in Narita by divine oracle, and the former shrine became the Okumiya. When it was recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, because "Makata no Ma" shared a name with one of the {{ill|lt=Three Sacred Treasures|Q327465|WD=Q327465|1=Imperial Regalia of Japan}}, one character was taken and the name was changed to "Makata Shrine." Later, because the area was a hemp-producing region, the shrine name was changed to "Asakata (Makata) Shrine." ファイル:Makata-jinjya-enkei.jpg|Panoramic view ファイル:Makata-jinjya-honden.jpg|Shrine buildings ファイル:Makata-jinjya-hondenura.jpg|Rear of main hall ファイル:Makata-jinjya-kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall ファイル:Amenohituku-jinjya.jpg|Ame-no-Hitsuku Shrine behind the main hall === Okumiya === Located in Teguros, Funagata, Narita, and called Teguro-sha. It is the inner shrine of Daikata. ファイル:Makatajinjya-zenkei.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Panoramic view ファイル:Makatajinjya-syadenzenkei.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Overall view of shrine buildings ファイル:Makatajinjya-syaden.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Shrine buildings ファイル:Makatajinjya-syaden2.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Shrine buildings from the rear ファイル:Makatajinjya-goshiboku.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Sacred tree (large cedar) ファイル:Makatajinjya-itukorinomikoto-funbo.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Traditional tomb-stone chamber of Itokori-no-Mikoto (Chiba Prefecture designated cultural property) ファイル:Makatajinjya-itukorinomikoto-funbo2.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Stone excavated from the traditional tomb of Itokori-no-Mikoto === Other Makata Shrines === Shrines named "Makata Shrine" number 18 in the {{ill|lt=Inba District|Q677245|WD=Q677245|lt=Inba area|1=Inba District}}: one in Narita including Daikata, eleven in {{ill|lt=Sakura|Q498011|WD=Q498011|1=Sakura, Chiba}}, two in {{ill|lt=Shisui|Q782157|WD=Q782157|1=Shisui}}, two in {{ill|lt=Tomisato|Q692857|WD=Q692857|1=Tomisato}}, and one in {{ill|lt=Yachiyo|Q328651|WD=Q328651|1=Yachiyo, Chiba}}. It is a rare shrine name nationwide, and these shrines exist only from the east side of {{ill|lt=Lake Inba|Q5120731|WD=Q5120731|1=Inbanuma}} southward. * {{ill|lt=Makata Shrine (Sakura, Kanaki-cho)|Q11677653|WD=Q11677653|1=Makata Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Makata Shrine (Shisui, Shisui)|Q24876715|WD=Q24876715|1=Makata- Shrine}} === Subsidiary shrines === === Main shrine precinct === ; Sessha * Amahitsuku Shrine{{efn|On {{ill|lt=June 10|Q2615|WD=Q2615|1=June 10}} {{ill|lt=1944|Q5268|WD=Q5268|1=1944}} (Showa 19), {{ill|lt=Amano Okamoto|Q11473104|WD=Q11473104|1=Tenmei Okamoto}} recorded a "Nichigetsu Shinji" ritual at the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine office|1=UNKNOWN}} of Makata Shrine.}} and Daigongen Shrine [[File:天日津久神社-本殿.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Amahitsuku Shrine}}]] [[File:麻賀多神社(本宮)-大権現社-本殿.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Daigongen Shrine}}]] ; Massha * Mihato Shrine - (enshrined deities) Seoritsuhime, Ibukidonushi, Hayakitsuhime, Hayasasurahime * Furumine Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine * Inba Kuni no Miyatsuko Shrine, Maki Tarojo Shrine * Sakitama Shrine, Aoma Shrine, Sarutahiko Shrine * Tenjin Shrine [[File:麻賀多神社-(本宮)-古峯神社・三峯神社-.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Furumine Shrine and Mitsumine Shrine}}]] === Okumiya === ; Sessha * Kashiwahime Shrine, Yasushiro Inari Shrine ; Massha * Takatsu Shrine, Kuriohi Shrine, Yonao Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Sugawara Shrine ; Shrine outside the precinct * Itokori Shrine - listed as "Itokori Shrine" by Jinja Honcho; the torii inscription reads "Kanahira Shrine" === Cultural properties === [[ファイル:Makatajinjya-koudunooosugi.jpg|thumb|200px|The great {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=cedar|1=UNKNOWN}} at Makata Shrine, also called "Kozu no Ōsugi."]] The following are designated {{ill|lt=cultural properties|Q858308|WD=Q858308|1=Cultural Property (Japan)}}{{Cite web|和書|author=Narita City Board of Education Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/bunka_sports/page215300.html|title=Notice and use of cultural properties (list of cultural properties)|work=Narita City, Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-04-24}}. * Prefecturally designated {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} ** Forest of Makata Shrine - designated {{ill|lt=March 8|Q2395|WD=Q2395|1=March 8}} {{ill|lt=1977|Q2481|WD=Q2481|1=1977}} (Showa 52). The designated area is 6,386 square meters of shrine grove land{{R|"CityBunkazai"}}. In addition to more than 20 {{ill|lt=cedar|Q147388|WD=Q147388|1=Cryptomeria}} trees, species such as {{ill|lt=oak|Q796117|WD=Q796117|1=Cyclobalanopsis}} and {{ill|lt=shii|Q1065400|WD=Q1065400|1=Castanopsis}} grow there. The "Kozu no Ōsugi," traditionally said to be 1,200-1,300 years old, is especially a giant tree about 40 meters tall with a trunk circumference of about 8 meters{{Cite web |和書 |url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p431-063.html |title=Forest of Makata Shrine / Chiba Prefecture |website=Official Chiba Prefecture Website (www.pref.chiba.lg.jp) |publisher=Chiba Prefectural Government |date=2024-02-27 |access-date=2025-04-21}}. * City-designated {{ill|lt=Tangible Cultural Property|Q2901860|WD=Q2901860|1=Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)}} ** Main hall of Makata Shrine - designated {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995|1=November 3}} {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475|1=1971}} (Showa 46). * City-designated {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Makata Shrine Kagura - designated {{ill|lt=October 6|Q2928|WD=Q2928|1=October 6}} {{ill|lt=1975|Q2479|WD=Q2479|1=1975}} (Showa 50). * City-designated {{ill|lt=Historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138|1=Historic site}} ** Shikinaisha Makata Shrine - designated November 3, {{ill|lt=1969|Q2485|WD=Q2485|1=1969}} (Showa 44). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Chiba Prefecture|Q11406137|WD=Q11406137|1=list of Shrines in Chiba Prefecture}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Makata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Makata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Narita City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Narita, Chiba]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Chiba Heritage Top 100]] [[Category:房総の魅力500選]][[Category:qq]]

Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|46|15.0|N|140|16|35.0|E}}}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Narita|Q273798|WD=Q273798|1=Narita, Chiba}} Daikata 1
834 Funagata, Narita, Chiba Prefecture (Okumiya) |緯度度=35|緯度分=46|緯度秒=15.0|N(北緯)及びS(南緯)=N |経度度=140|経度分=16|経度秒=35.0|E(東経)及びW(西経)=E |祭神={{ill|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=Wakumusubi}}
{{ill|lt=Wakahirume-no-Mikoto|Q11596349|WD=Q11596349|lt=Wakahirume-no-Mikoto|1=Wakahiru-me}} (Okumiya) |社格=Shikinaisha (small) / gosha |本殿=One-bay shrine |創建=42nd year of Emperor Keiko |例祭=July 31 | 地図国コード = JP |地図=Japan Chiba |ラベル位置=bottom }} '''Makata Shrine''' (まかたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Daikata, {{ill|lt=Narita|Q273798|WD=Q273798|1=Narita, Chiba}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is the head shrine of the "18 Makata Shrines" (Makata Juhassha) in the {{ill|lt=Inba District area|Q11409099|WD=Q11409099|1=Inba District area}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 42nd year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} (AD 113), {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} visited this area while on his eastern campaign, hung a mirror on a cedar trunk, and said: "If you revere and worship this mirror as Inba-no-Kunitama-Okitsukagami, the five grains will be abundant." This is said to be the origin of the shrine. In the 20th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, Itokori-no-Mikoto, the 8th-generation descendant of {{ill|lt=Kamuyatsumimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} and the {{ill|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko of Inba|Q11409105|WD=Q11409105|lt=Inba no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Inba no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, built a shrine hall at present-day Funagata in Narita and enshrined Wakahirume-no-Mikoto with that mirror as the {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}. Itokori-no-Mikoto also dug up seven jewels from beneath the cedar and additionally enshrined Wakumusubi-no-Kami with them as shintai. These two deities were called the "great deities of Makata no Ma" (magatama). In the 16th year of {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} (AD 608), Hirokushi Tekurohiko-no-Mikoto, the 8th-generation descendant of Itokori-no-Mikoto, transferred Wakumusubi-no-Kami to present-day Daikata in Narita by divine oracle, and the former shrine became the Okumiya. When it was recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, because "Makata no Ma" shared a name with one of the {{ill|lt=Three Sacred Treasures|Q327465|WD=Q327465|1=Imperial Regalia of Japan}}, one character was taken and the name was changed to "Makata Shrine." Later, because the area was a hemp-producing region, the shrine name was changed to "Asakata (Makata) Shrine." ファイル:Makata-jinjya-enkei.jpg|Panoramic view ファイル:Makata-jinjya-honden.jpg|Shrine buildings ファイル:Makata-jinjya-hondenura.jpg|Rear of main hall ファイル:Makata-jinjya-kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall ファイル:Amenohituku-jinjya.jpg|Ame-no-Hitsuku Shrine behind the main hall === Okumiya === Located in Teguros, Funagata, Narita, and called Teguro-sha. It is the inner shrine of Daikata. ファイル:Makatajinjya-zenkei.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Panoramic view ファイル:Makatajinjya-syadenzenkei.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Overall view of shrine buildings ファイル:Makatajinjya-syaden.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Shrine buildings ファイル:Makatajinjya-syaden2.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Shrine buildings from the rear ファイル:Makatajinjya-goshiboku.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Sacred tree (large cedar) ファイル:Makatajinjya-itukorinomikoto-funbo.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Traditional tomb-stone chamber of Itokori-no-Mikoto (Chiba Prefecture designated cultural property) ファイル:Makatajinjya-itukorinomikoto-funbo2.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Stone excavated from the traditional tomb of Itokori-no-Mikoto === Other Makata Shrines === Shrines named "Makata Shrine" number 18 in the {{ill|lt=Inba District|Q677245|WD=Q677245|lt=Inba area|1=Inba District}}: one in Narita including Daikata, eleven in {{ill|lt=Sakura|Q498011|WD=Q498011|1=Sakura, Chiba}}, two in {{ill|lt=Shisui|Q782157|WD=Q782157|1=Shisui}}, two in {{ill|lt=Tomisato|Q692857|WD=Q692857|1=Tomisato}}, and one in {{ill|lt=Yachiyo|Q328651|WD=Q328651|1=Yachiyo, Chiba}}. It is a rare shrine name nationwide, and these shrines exist only from the east side of {{ill|lt=Lake Inba|Q5120731|WD=Q5120731|1=Inbanuma}} southward. * {{ill|lt=Makata Shrine (Sakura, Kanaki-cho)|Q11677653|WD=Q11677653|1=Makata Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Makata Shrine (Shisui, Shisui)|Q24876715|WD=Q24876715|1=Makata- Shrine}} === Subsidiary shrines === === Main shrine precinct === ; Sessha * Amahitsuku Shrine{{efn|On {{ill|lt=June 10|Q2615|WD=Q2615|1=June 10}} {{ill|lt=1944|Q5268|WD=Q5268|1=1944}} (Showa 19), {{ill|lt=Amano Okamoto|Q11473104|WD=Q11473104|1=Tenmei Okamoto}} recorded a "Nichigetsu Shinji" ritual at the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine office|1=UNKNOWN}} of Makata Shrine.}} and Daigongen Shrine [[File:天日津久神社-本殿.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Amahitsuku Shrine}}]] [[File:麻賀多神社(本宮)-大権現社-本殿.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Daigongen Shrine}}]] ; Massha * Mihato Shrine - (enshrined deities) Seoritsuhime, Ibukidonushi, Hayakitsuhime, Hayasasurahime * Furumine Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine * Inba Kuni no Miyatsuko Shrine, Maki Tarojo Shrine * Sakitama Shrine, Aoma Shrine, Sarutahiko Shrine * Tenjin Shrine [[File:麻賀多神社-(本宮)-古峯神社・三峯神社-.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Furumine Shrine and Mitsumine Shrine}}]] === Okumiya === ; Sessha * Kashiwahime Shrine, Yasushiro Inari Shrine ; Massha * Takatsu Shrine, Kuriohi Shrine, Yonao Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Sugawara Shrine ; Shrine outside the precinct * Itokori Shrine - listed as "Itokori Shrine" by Jinja Honcho; the torii inscription reads "Kanahira Shrine" === Cultural properties === [[ファイル:Makatajinjya-koudunooosugi.jpg|thumb|200px|The great {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=cedar|1=UNKNOWN}} at Makata Shrine, also called "Kozu no Ōsugi."]] The following are designated {{ill|lt=cultural properties|Q858308|WD=Q858308|1=Cultural Property (Japan)}}{{Cite web|和書|author=Narita City Board of Education Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/bunka_sports/page215300.html|title=Notice and use of cultural properties (list of cultural properties)|work=Narita City, Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-04-24}}. * Prefecturally designated {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} ** Forest of Makata Shrine - designated {{ill|lt=March 8|Q2395|WD=Q2395|1=March 8}} {{ill|lt=1977|Q2481|WD=Q2481|1=1977}} (Showa 52). The designated area is 6,386 square meters of shrine grove land{{R|"CityBunkazai"}}. In addition to more than 20 {{ill|lt=cedar|Q147388|WD=Q147388|1=Cryptomeria}} trees, species such as {{ill|lt=oak|Q796117|WD=Q796117|1=Cyclobalanopsis}} and {{ill|lt=shii|Q1065400|WD=Q1065400|1=Castanopsis}} grow there. The "Kozu no Ōsugi," traditionally said to be 1,200-1,300 years old, is especially a giant tree about 40 meters tall with a trunk circumference of about 8 meters{{Cite web |和書 |url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p431-063.html |title=Forest of Makata Shrine / Chiba Prefecture |website=Official Chiba Prefecture Website (www.pref.chiba.lg.jp) |publisher=Chiba Prefectural Government |date=2024-02-27 |access-date=2025-04-21}}. * City-designated {{ill|lt=Tangible Cultural Property|Q2901860|WD=Q2901860|1=Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)}} ** Main hall of Makata Shrine - designated {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995|1=November 3}} {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475|1=1971}} (Showa 46). * City-designated {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Makata Shrine Kagura - designated {{ill|lt=October 6|Q2928|WD=Q2928|1=October 6}} {{ill|lt=1975|Q2479|WD=Q2479|1=1975}} (Showa 50). * City-designated {{ill|lt=Historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138|1=Historic site}} ** Shikinaisha Makata Shrine - designated November 3, {{ill|lt=1969|Q2485|WD=Q2485|1=1969}} (Showa 44). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Chiba Prefecture|Q11406137|WD=Q11406137|1=list of Shrines in Chiba Prefecture}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Makata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Makata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Narita City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Narita, Chiba]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Chiba Heritage Top 100]] [[Category:Top 500 Attractions of Bōsō Peninsula]][[Category:qq]]

It is the head shrine of the "18 Makata Shrines" (Makata Juhassha) in the {{ill|lt=Inba District area|Q11409099|WD=Q11409099|1=Inba District area}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 42nd year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} (AD 113), {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} visited this area while on his eastern campaign, hung a mirror on a cedar trunk, and said: "If you revere and worship this mirror as Inba-no-Kunitama-Okitsukagami, the five grains will be abundant." This is said to be the origin of the shrine. In the 20th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, Itokori-no-Mikoto, the 8th-generation descendant of {{ill|lt=Kamuyatsumimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} and the {{ill|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko of Inba|Q11409105|WD=Q11409105|lt=Inba no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Inba no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, built a shrine hall at present-day Funagata in Narita and enshrined Wakahirume-no-Mikoto with that mirror as the {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}. Itokori-no-Mikoto also dug up seven jewels from beneath the cedar and additionally enshrined Wakumusubi-no-Kami with them as shintai. These two deities were called the "great deities of Makata no Ma" (magatama). In the 16th year of {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} (AD 608), Hirokushi Tekurohiko-no-Mikoto, the 8th-generation descendant of Itokori-no-Mikoto, transferred Wakumusubi-no-Kami to present-day Daikata in Narita by divine oracle, and the former shrine became the Okumiya. When it was recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, because "Makata no Ma" shared a name with one of the {{ill|lt=Three Sacred Treasures|Q327465|WD=Q327465|1=Imperial Regalia of Japan}}, one character was taken and the name was changed to "Makata Shrine." Later, because the area was a hemp-producing region, the shrine name was changed to "Asakata (Makata) Shrine." ファイル:Makata-jinjya-enkei.jpg|Panoramic view ファイル:Makata-jinjya-honden.jpg|Shrine buildings ファイル:Makata-jinjya-hondenura.jpg|Rear of main hall ファイル:Makata-jinjya-kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall ファイル:Amenohituku-jinjya.jpg|Ame-no-Hitsuku Shrine behind the main hall === Okumiya === Located in Teguros, Funagata, Narita, and called Teguro-sha. It is the inner shrine of Daikata. ファイル:Makatajinjya-zenkei.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Panoramic view ファイル:Makatajinjya-syadenzenkei.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Overall view of shrine buildings ファイル:Makatajinjya-syaden.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Shrine buildings ファイル:Makatajinjya-syaden2.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Shrine buildings from the rear ファイル:Makatajinjya-goshiboku.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Sacred tree (large cedar) ファイル:Makatajinjya-itukorinomikoto-funbo.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Traditional tomb-stone chamber of Itokori-no-Mikoto (Chiba Prefecture designated cultural property) ファイル:Makatajinjya-itukorinomikoto-funbo2.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Stone excavated from the traditional tomb of Itokori-no-Mikoto === Other Makata Shrines === Shrines named "Makata Shrine" number 18 in the {{ill|lt=Inba District|Q677245|WD=Q677245|lt=Inba area|1=Inba District}}: one in Narita including Daikata, eleven in {{ill|lt=Sakura|Q498011|WD=Q498011|1=Sakura, Chiba}}, two in {{ill|lt=Shisui|Q782157|WD=Q782157|1=Shisui}}, two in {{ill|lt=Tomisato|Q692857|WD=Q692857|1=Tomisato}}, and one in {{ill|lt=Yachiyo|Q328651|WD=Q328651|1=Yachiyo, Chiba}}. It is a rare shrine name nationwide, and these shrines exist only from the east side of {{ill|lt=Lake Inba|Q5120731|WD=Q5120731|1=Inbanuma}} southward. * {{ill|lt=Makata Shrine (Sakura, Kanaki-cho)|Q11677653|WD=Q11677653|1=Makata Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Makata Shrine (Shisui, Shisui)|Q24876715|WD=Q24876715|1=Makata- Shrine}} === Subsidiary shrines === === Main shrine precinct === ; Sessha * Amahitsuku Shrine{{efn|On {{ill|lt=June 10|Q2615|WD=Q2615|1=June 10}} {{ill|lt=1944|Q5268|WD=Q5268|1=1944}} (Showa 19), {{ill|lt=Amano Okamoto|Q11473104|WD=Q11473104|1=Tenmei Okamoto}} recorded a "Nichigetsu Shinji" ritual at the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine office|1=UNKNOWN}} of Makata Shrine.}} and Daigongen Shrine [[File:天日津久神社-本殿.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Amahitsuku Shrine}}]] [[File:麻賀多神社(本宮)-大権現社-本殿.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Daigongen Shrine}}]] ; Massha * Mihato Shrine - (enshrined deities) Seoritsuhime, Ibukidonushi, Hayakitsuhime, Hayasasurahime * Furumine Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine * Inba Kuni no Miyatsuko Shrine, Maki Tarojo Shrine * Sakitama Shrine, Aoma Shrine, Sarutahiko Shrine * Tenjin Shrine [[File:麻賀多神社-(本宮)-古峯神社・三峯神社-.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Furumine Shrine and Mitsumine Shrine}}]] === Okumiya === ; Sessha * Kashiwahime Shrine, Yasushiro Inari Shrine ; Massha * Takatsu Shrine, Kuriohi Shrine, Yonao Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Sugawara Shrine ; Shrine outside the precinct * Itokori Shrine - listed as "Itokori Shrine" by Jinja Honcho; the torii inscription reads "Kanahira Shrine" === Cultural properties === [[ファイル:Makatajinjya-koudunooosugi.jpg|thumb|200px|The great {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=cedar|1=UNKNOWN}} at Makata Shrine, also called "Kozu no Ōsugi."]] The following are designated {{ill|lt=cultural properties|Q858308|WD=Q858308|1=Cultural Property (Japan)}}{{Cite web|和書|author=Narita City Board of Education Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/bunka_sports/page215300.html|title=Notice and use of cultural properties (list of cultural properties)|work=Narita City, Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-04-24}}. * Prefecturally designated {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} ** Forest of Makata Shrine - designated {{ill|lt=March 8|Q2395|WD=Q2395|1=March 8}} {{ill|lt=1977|Q2481|WD=Q2481|1=1977}} (Showa 52). The designated area is 6,386 square meters of shrine grove land{{R|"CityBunkazai"}}. In addition to more than 20 {{ill|lt=cedar|Q147388|WD=Q147388|1=Cryptomeria}} trees, species such as {{ill|lt=oak|Q796117|WD=Q796117|1=Cyclobalanopsis}} and {{ill|lt=shii|Q1065400|WD=Q1065400|1=Castanopsis}} grow there. The "Kozu no Ōsugi," traditionally said to be 1,200-1,300 years old, is especially a giant tree about 40 meters tall with a trunk circumference of about 8 meters{{Cite web |和書 |url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p431-063.html |title=Forest of Makata Shrine / Chiba Prefecture |website=Official Chiba Prefecture Website (www.pref.chiba.lg.jp) |publisher=Chiba Prefectural Government |date=2024-02-27 |access-date=2025-04-21}}. * City-designated {{ill|lt=Tangible Cultural Property|Q2901860|WD=Q2901860|1=Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)}} ** Main hall of Makata Shrine - designated {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995|1=November 3}} {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475|1=1971}} (Showa 46). * City-designated {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Makata Shrine Kagura - designated {{ill|lt=October 6|Q2928|WD=Q2928|1=October 6}} {{ill|lt=1975|Q2479|WD=Q2479|1=1975}} (Showa 50). * City-designated {{ill|lt=Historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138|1=Historic site}} ** Shikinaisha Makata Shrine - designated November 3, {{ill|lt=1969|Q2485|WD=Q2485|1=1969}} (Showa 44). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Chiba Prefecture|Q11406137|WD=Q11406137|1=list of Shrines in Chiba Prefecture}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Makata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Makata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Narita City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Narita, Chiba]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Chiba Heritage Top 100]] [[Category:Top 500 Attractions of Bōsō Peninsula]]

It is a rare shrine name nationwide, and these shrines exist only from the east side of {{ill|lt=Lake Inba|Q5120731|WD=Q5120731|1=Inbanuma}} southward. * {{ill|lt=Makata Shrine (Sakura, Kanaki-cho)|Q11677653|WD=Q11677653|1=Makata Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Makata Shrine (Shisui, Shisui)|Q24876715|WD=Q24876715|1=Makata- Shrine}} === Subsidiary shrines === === Main shrine precinct === ; Sessha * Amahitsuku Shrine{{efn|On {{ill|lt=June 10|Q2615|WD=Q2615|1=June 10}} {{ill|lt=1944|Q5268|WD=Q5268|1=1944}} (Showa 19), {{ill|lt=Amano Okamoto|Q11473104|WD=Q11473104|1=Tenmei Okamoto}} recorded a "Nichigetsu Shinji" ritual at the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine office|1=UNKNOWN}} of Makata Shrine.}} and Daigongen Shrine [[File:天日津久神社-本殿.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Amahitsuku Shrine}}]] [[File:麻賀多神社(本宮)-大権現社-本殿.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Daigongen Shrine}}]] ; Massha * Mihato Shrine - (enshrined deities) Seoritsuhime, Ibukidonushi, Hayakitsuhime, Hayasasurahime * Furumine Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine * Inba Kuni no Miyatsuko Shrine, Maki Tarojo Shrine * Sakitama Shrine, Aoma Shrine, Sarutahiko Shrine * Tenjin Shrine [[File:麻賀多神社-(本宮)-古峯神社・三峯神社-.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Furumine Shrine and Mitsumine Shrine}}]] === Okumiya === ; Sessha * Kashiwahime Shrine, Yasushiro Inari Shrine ; Massha * Takatsu Shrine, Kuriohi Shrine, Yonao Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Sugawara Shrine ; Shrine outside the precinct * Itokori Shrine - listed as "Itokori Shrine" by Jinja Honcho; the torii inscription reads "Kanahira Shrine" === Cultural properties === [[ファイル:Makatajinjya-koudunooosugi.jpg|thumb|200px|The great {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=cedar|1=UNKNOWN}} at Makata Shrine, also called "Kozu no Ōsugi."]] The following are designated {{ill|lt=cultural properties|Q858308|WD=Q858308|1=Cultural Property (Japan)}}{{Cite web|和書|author=Narita City Board of Education Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/bunka_sports/page215300.html|title=Notice and use of cultural properties (list of cultural properties)|work=Narita City, Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-04-24}}. * Prefecturally designated {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} ** Forest of Makata Shrine - designated {{ill|lt=March 8|Q2395|WD=Q2395|1=March 8}} {{ill|lt=1977|Q2481|WD=Q2481|1=1977}} (Showa 52). The designated area is 6,386 square meters of shrine grove land{{R|"CityBunkazai"}}. In addition to more than 20 {{ill|lt=cedar|Q147388|WD=Q147388|1=Cryptomeria}} trees, species such as {{ill|lt=oak|Q796117|WD=Q796117|1=Cyclobalanopsis}} and {{ill|lt=shii|Q1065400|WD=Q1065400|1=Castanopsis}} grow there. The "Kozu no Ōsugi," traditionally said to be 1,200-1,300 years old, is especially a giant tree about 40 meters tall with a trunk circumference of about 8 meters{{Cite web |和書 |url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p431-063.html |title=Forest of Makata Shrine / Chiba Prefecture |website=Official Chiba Prefecture Website (www.pref.chiba.lg.jp) |publisher=Chiba Prefectural Government |date=2024-02-27 |access-date=2025-04-21}}. * City-designated {{ill|lt=Tangible Cultural Property|Q2901860|WD=Q2901860|1=Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)}} ** Main hall of Makata Shrine - designated {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995|1=November 3}} {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475|1=1971}} (Showa 46). * City-designated {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Makata Shrine Kagura - designated {{ill|lt=October 6|Q2928|WD=Q2928|1=October 6}} {{ill|lt=1975|Q2479|WD=Q2479|1=1975}} (Showa 50). * City-designated {{ill|lt=Historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138|1=Historic site}} ** Shikinaisha Makata Shrine - designated November 3, {{ill|lt=1969|Q2485|WD=Q2485|1=1969}} (Showa 44). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Chiba Prefecture|Q11406137|WD=Q11406137|1=list of Shrines in Chiba Prefecture}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Makata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Makata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Narita City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Narita, Chiba]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Chiba Heritage Top 100]] [[Category:Top 500 Attractions of Bōsō Peninsula]]

Prefecture|other Makata shrines|Makata Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{出典の明記|date=2016-12-18}} {{神社 |名称=Makata Shrine |画像=[[ファイル:Makatajinjya-hondou.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|46|15.0|N|140|16|35.0|E}}}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|qid=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Narita|Q273798|qid=Q273798|1=Narita, Chiba}} Daikata 1
834 Funagata, Narita, Chiba Prefecture (Okumiya) |緯度度=35|緯度分=46|緯度秒=15.0|N(北緯)及びS(南緯)=N |経度度=140|経度分=16|経度秒=35.0|E(東経)及びW(西経)=E |祭神={{ill|qid=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|qid=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=Wakumusubi}}
{{ill|lt=Wakahirume-no-Mikoto|Q11596349|qid=Q11596349|lt=Wakahirume-no-Mikoto|1=Wakahiru-me}} (Okumiya) |社格=Shikinaisha (small) / gosha |本殿=One-bay shrine |創建=42nd year of Emperor Keiko |例祭=July 31 | 地図国コード = JP |地図=Japan Chiba |ラベル位置=bottom }} '''Makata Shrine''' (まかたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Daikata, {{ill|lt=Narita|Q273798|qid=Q273798|1=Narita, Chiba}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|qid=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|qid=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is the head shrine of the "18 Makata Shrines" (Makata Juhassha) in the {{ill|lt=Inba District area|Q11409099|qid=Q11409099|1=Inba District area}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 42nd year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|qid=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} (AD 113), {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} visited this area while on his eastern campaign, hung a mirror on a cedar trunk, and said: "If you revere and worship this mirror as Inba-no-Kunitama-Okitsukagami, the five grains will be abundant." This is said to be the origin of the shrine. In the 20th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, Itokori-no-Mikoto, the 8th-generation descendant of {{ill|lt=Kamuyatsumimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|qid=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} and the {{ill|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko of Inba|Q11409105|qid=Q11409105|lt=Inba no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Inba no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, built a shrine hall at present-day Funagata in Narita and enshrined Wakahirume-no-Mikoto with that mirror as the {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|qid=Q327532|1=Shintai}}. Itokori-no-Mikoto also dug up seven jewels from beneath the cedar and additionally enshrined Wakumusubi-no-Kami with them as shintai. These two deities were called the "great deities of Makata no Ma" (magatama). In the 16th year of {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|qid=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} (AD 608), Hirokushi Tekurohiko-no-Mikoto, the 8th-generation descendant of Itokori-no-Mikoto, transferred Wakumusubi-no-Kami to present-day Daikata in Narita by divine oracle, and the former shrine became the Okumiya. When it was recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, because "Makata no Ma" shared a name with one of the {{ill|lt=Three Sacred Treasures|Q327465|qid=Q327465|1=Imperial Regalia of Japan}}, one character was taken and the name was changed to "Makata Shrine." Later, because the area was a hemp-producing region, the shrine name was changed to "Asakata (Makata) Shrine." ファイル:Makata-jinjya-enkei.jpg|Panoramic view ファイル:Makata-jinjya-honden.jpg|Shrine buildings ファイル:Makata-jinjya-hondenura.jpg|Rear of main hall ファイル:Makata-jinjya-kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall ファイル:Amenohituku-jinjya.jpg|Ame-no-Hitsuku Shrine behind the main hall === Okumiya === Located in Teguros, Funagata, Narita, and called Teguro-sha. It is the inner shrine of Daikata. ファイル:Makatajinjya-zenkei.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Panoramic view ファイル:Makatajinjya-syadenzenkei.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Overall view of shrine buildings ファイル:Makatajinjya-syaden.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Shrine buildings ファイル:Makatajinjya-syaden2.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Shrine buildings from the rear ファイル:Makatajinjya-goshiboku.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Sacred tree (large cedar) ファイル:Makatajinjya-itukorinomikoto-funbo.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Traditional tomb-stone chamber of Itokori-no-Mikoto (Chiba Prefecture designated cultural property) ファイル:Makatajinjya-itukorinomikoto-funbo2.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Stone excavated from the traditional tomb of Itokori-no-Mikoto === Other Makata Shrines === Shrines named "Makata Shrine" number 18 in the {{ill|lt=Inba District|Q677245|qid=Q677245|lt=Inba area|1=Inba District}}: one in Narita including Daikata, eleven in {{ill|lt=Sakura|Q498011|qid=Q498011|1=Sakura, Chiba}}, two in {{ill|lt=Shisui|Q782157|qid=Q782157|1=Shisui}}, two in {{ill|lt=Tomisato|Q692857|qid=Q692857|1=Tomisato}}, and one in {{ill|lt=Yachiyo|Q328651|qid=Q328651|1=Yachiyo, Chiba}}. It is a rare shrine name nationwide, and these shrines exist only from the east side of {{ill|lt=Lake Inba|Q5120731|qid=Q5120731|1=Inbanuma}} southward. * {{ill|lt=Makata Shrine (Sakura, Kanaki-cho)|Q11677653|qid=Q11677653|1=Makata Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Makata Shrine (Shisui, Shisui)|Q24876715|qid=Q24876715|1=Makata- Shrine}} === Subsidiary shrines === === Main shrine precinct === ; Sessha * Amahitsuku Shrine{{efn|On {{ill|lt=June 10|Q2615|qid=Q2615|1=June 10}} {{ill|lt=1944|Q5268|qid=Q5268|1=1944}} (Showa 19), {{ill|lt=Amano Okamoto|Q11473104|qid=Q11473104|1=Tenmei Okamoto}} recorded a "Nichigetsu Shinji" ritual at the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine office|1=UNKNOWN}} of Makata Shrine.}} and Daigongen Shrine [[File:天日津久神社-本殿.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Amahitsuku Shrine}}]] [[File:麻賀多神社(本宮)-大権現社-本殿.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Daigongen Shrine}}]] ; Sessha * Kashiwahime Shrine, Yasushiro Inari Shrine ; Massha * Takatsu Shrine, Kuriohi Shrine, Yonao Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Sugawara Shrine ; Shrine outside the precinct * Itokori Shrine - listed as "Itokori Shrine" by Jinja Honcho; the torii inscription reads "Kanahira Shrine" === Cultural properties === [[ファイル:Makatajinjya-koudunooosugi.jpg|thumb|200px|The great {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=cedar|1=UNKNOWN}} at Makata Shrine, also called "Kozu no Ōsugi."]] The following are designated {{ill|lt=cultural properties|Q858308|qid=Q858308|1=Cultural Property (Japan)}}{{Cite web|和書|author=Narita City Board of Education Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/bunka_sports/page215300.html|title=Notice and use of cultural properties (list of cultural properties)|work=Narita City, Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-04-24}}. * Prefecturally designated {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|qid=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} ** Forest of Makata Shrine - designated {{ill|lt=March 8|Q2395|qid=Q2395|1=March 8}} {{ill|lt=1977|Q2481|qid=Q2481|1=1977}} (Showa 52). The designated area is 6,386 square meters of shrine grove land{{R|"CityBunkazai"}}. In addition to more than 20 {{ill|lt=cedar|Q147388|qid=Q147388|1=Cryptomeria}} trees, species such as {{ill|lt=oak|Q796117|qid=Q796117|1=Cyclobalanopsis}} and {{ill|lt=shii|Q1065400|qid=Q1065400|1=Castanopsis}} grow there. The "Kozu no Ōsugi," traditionally said to be 1,200-1,300 years old, is especially a giant tree about 40 meters tall with a trunk circumference of about 8 meters{{Cite web |和書 |url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p431-063.html |title=Forest of Makata Shrine / Chiba Prefecture |website=Official Chiba Prefecture Website (www.pref.chiba.lg.jp) |publisher=Chiba Prefectural Government |date=2024-02-27 |access-date=2025-04-21}}. * City-designated {{ill|lt=Tangible Cultural Property|Q2901860|qid=Q2901860|1=Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)}} ** Main hall of Makata Shrine - designated {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|qid=Q2995|1=November 3}} {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|qid=Q2475|1=1971}} (Showa 46). * City-designated {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Makata Shrine Kagura - designated {{ill|lt=October 6|Q2928|qid=Q2928|1=October 6}} {{ill|lt=1975|Q2479|qid=Q2479|1=1975}} (Showa 50). * City-designated {{ill|lt=Historic site|Q1081138|qid=Q1081138|1=Historic site}} ** Shikinaisha Makata Shrine - designated November 3, {{ill|lt=1969|Q2485|qid=Q2485|1=1969}} (Showa 44). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Chiba Prefecture|Q11406137|qid=Q11406137|1=list of Shrines in Chiba Prefecture}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Makata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Makata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Narita City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Narita, Chiba]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Chiba Heritage Top 100]] [[Category:Top 500 Attractions of Bōsō Peninsula]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|46|15.0|N|140|16|35.0|E}}}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|qid=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Narita|Q273798|qid=Q273798|1=Narita, Chiba}} Daikata 1
834 Funagata, Narita, Chiba Prefecture (Okumiya) |緯度度=35|緯度分=46|緯度秒=15.0|N(北緯)及びS(南緯)=N |経度度=140|経度分=16|経度秒=35.0|E(東経)及びW(西経)=E |祭神={{ill|qid=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|qid=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Kami|1=Wakumusubi}}
{{ill|lt=Wakahirume-no-Mikoto|Q11596349|qid=Q11596349|lt=Wakahirume-no-Mikoto|1=Wakahiru-me}} (Okumiya) |社格=Shikinaisha (small) / gosha |本殿=One-bay shrine |創建=42nd year of Emperor Keiko |例祭=July 31 | 地図国コード = JP |地図=Japan Chiba |ラベル位置=bottom }} '''Makata Shrine''' (まかたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Daikata, {{ill|lt=Narita|Q273798|qid=Q273798|1=Narita, Chiba}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|qid=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|qid=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is the head shrine of the "18 Makata Shrines" (Makata Juhassha) in the {{ill|lt=Inba District area|Q11409099|qid=Q11409099|1=Inba District area}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 42nd year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|qid=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} (AD 113), {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} visited this area while on his eastern campaign, hung a mirror on a cedar trunk, and said: "If you revere and worship this mirror as Inba-no-Kunitama-Okitsukagami, the five grains will be abundant." This is said to be the origin of the shrine. In the 20th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, Itokori-no-Mikoto, the 8th-generation descendant of {{ill|lt=Kamuyatsumimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|qid=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} and the {{ill|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko of Inba|Q11409105|qid=Q11409105|lt=Inba no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Inba no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, built a shrine hall at present-day Funagata in Narita and enshrined Wakahirume-no-Mikoto with that mirror as the {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|qid=Q327532|1=Shintai}}. Itokori-no-Mikoto also dug up seven jewels from beneath the cedar and additionally enshrined Wakumusubi-no-Kami with them as shintai. These two deities were called the "great deities of Makata no Ma" (magatama). In the 16th year of {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|qid=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} (AD 608), Hirokushi Tekurohiko-no-Mikoto, the 8th-generation descendant of Itokori-no-Mikoto, transferred Wakumusubi-no-Kami to present-day Daikata in Narita by divine oracle, and the former shrine became the Okumiya. When it was recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, because "Makata no Ma" shared a name with one of the {{ill|lt=Three Sacred Treasures|Q327465|qid=Q327465|1=Imperial Regalia of Japan}}, one character was taken and the name was changed to "Makata Shrine." Later, because the area was a hemp-producing region, the shrine name was changed to "Asakata (Makata) Shrine." ファイル:Makata-jinjya-enkei.jpg|Panoramic view ファイル:Makata-jinjya-honden.jpg|Shrine buildings ファイル:Makata-jinjya-hondenura.jpg|Rear of main hall ファイル:Makata-jinjya-kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall ファイル:Amenohituku-jinjya.jpg|Ame-no-Hitsuku Shrine behind the main hall === Okumiya === Located in Teguros, Funagata, Narita, and called Teguro-sha. It is the inner shrine of Daikata. ファイル:Makatajinjya-zenkei.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Panoramic view ファイル:Makatajinjya-syadenzenkei.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Overall view of shrine buildings ファイル:Makatajinjya-syaden.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Shrine buildings ファイル:Makatajinjya-syaden2.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Shrine buildings from the rear ファイル:Makatajinjya-goshiboku.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Sacred tree (large cedar) ファイル:Makatajinjya-itukorinomikoto-funbo.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Traditional tomb-stone chamber of Itokori-no-Mikoto (Chiba Prefecture designated cultural property) ファイル:Makatajinjya-itukorinomikoto-funbo2.funagata-narita-city-,japan.jpg|Stone excavated from the traditional tomb of Itokori-no-Mikoto === Other Makata Shrines === Shrines named "Makata Shrine" number 18 in the {{ill|lt=Inba District|Q677245|qid=Q677245|lt=Inba area|1=Inba District}}: one in Narita including Daikata, eleven in {{ill|lt=Sakura|Q498011|qid=Q498011|1=Sakura, Chiba}}, two in {{ill|lt=Shisui|Q782157|qid=Q782157|1=Shisui}}, two in {{ill|lt=Tomisato|Q692857|qid=Q692857|1=Tomisato}}, and one in {{ill|lt=Yachiyo|Q328651|qid=Q328651|1=Yachiyo, Chiba}}. It is a rare shrine name nationwide, and these shrines exist only from the east side of {{ill|lt=Lake Inba|Q5120731|qid=Q5120731|1=Inbanuma}} southward. * {{ill|lt=Makata Shrine (Sakura, Kanaki-cho)|Q11677653|qid=Q11677653|1=Makata Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Makata Shrine (Shisui, Shisui)|Q24876715|qid=Q24876715|1=Makata- Shrine}} === Subsidiary shrines === === Main shrine precinct === ; Sessha * Amahitsuku Shrine{{efn|On {{ill|lt=June 10|Q2615|qid=Q2615|1=June 10}} {{ill|lt=1944|Q5268|qid=Q5268|1=1944}} (Showa 19), {{ill|lt=Amano Okamoto|Q11473104|qid=Q11473104|1=Tenmei Okamoto}} recorded a "Nichigetsu Shinji" ritual at the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine office|1=UNKNOWN}} of Makata Shrine.}} and Daigongen Shrine [[File:天日津久神社-本殿.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Amahitsuku Shrine}}]] [[File:麻賀多神社(本宮)-大権現社-本殿.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Daigongen Shrine}}]] ; Massha * Mihato Shrine - (enshrined deities) Seoritsuhime, Ibukidonushi, Hayakitsuhime, Hayasasurahime * Furumine Shrine, Mitsumine Shrine * Inba Kuni no Miyatsuko Shrine, Maki Tarojo Shrine * Sakitama Shrine, Aoma Shrine, Sarutahiko Shrine * Tenjin Shrine [[File:麻賀多神社-(本宮)-古峯神社・三峯神社-.jpg|thumb|{{ja|1=Furumine Shrine and Mitsumine Shrine}}]] === Okumiya === ; Sessha * Kashiwahime Shrine, Yasushiro Inari Shrine ; Massha * Takatsu Shrine, Kuriohi Shrine, Yonao Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Sugawara Shrine ; Shrine outside the precinct * Itokori Shrine - listed as "Itokori Shrine" by Jinja Honcho; the torii inscription reads "Kanahira Shrine" === Cultural properties === [[ファイル:Makatajinjya-koudunooosugi.jpg|thumb|200px|The great {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=cedar|1=UNKNOWN}} at Makata Shrine, also called "Kozu no Ōsugi."]] The following are designated {{ill|lt=cultural properties|Q858308|qid=Q858308|1=Cultural Property (Japan)}}{{Cite web|和書|author=Narita City Board of Education Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.narita.chiba.jp/bunka_sports/page215300.html|title=Notice and use of cultural properties (list of cultural properties)|work=Narita City, Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-04-24}}. * Prefecturally designated {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|qid=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} ** Forest of Makata Shrine - designated {{ill|lt=March 8|Q2395|qid=Q2395|1=March 8}} {{ill|lt=1977|Q2481|qid=Q2481|1=1977}} (Showa 52). The designated area is 6,386 square meters of shrine grove land{{R|"CityBunkazai"}}. In addition to more than 20 {{ill|lt=cedar|Q147388|qid=Q147388|1=Cryptomeria}} trees, species such as {{ill|lt=oak|Q796117|qid=Q796117|1=Cyclobalanopsis}} and {{ill|lt=shii|Q1065400|qid=Q1065400|1=Castanopsis}} grow there. The "Kozu no Ōsugi," traditionally said to be 1,200-1,300 years old, is especially a giant tree about 40 meters tall with a trunk circumference of about 8 meters{{Cite web |和書 |url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p431-063.html |title=Forest of Makata Shrine / Chiba Prefecture |website=Official Chiba Prefecture Website (www.pref.chiba.lg.jp) |publisher=Chiba Prefectural Government |date=2024-02-27 |access-date=2025-04-21}}. * City-designated {{ill|lt=Tangible Cultural Property|Q2901860|qid=Q2901860|1=Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)}} ** Main hall of Makata Shrine - designated {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|qid=Q2995|1=November 3}} {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|qid=Q2475|1=1971}} (Showa 46). * City-designated {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Makata Shrine Kagura - designated {{ill|lt=October 6|Q2928|qid=Q2928|1=October 6}} {{ill|lt=1975|Q2479|qid=Q2479|1=1975}} (Showa 50). * City-designated {{ill|lt=Historic site|Q1081138|qid=Q1081138|1=Historic site}} ** Shikinaisha Makata Shrine - designated November 3, {{ill|lt=1969|Q2485|qid=Q2485|1=1969}} (Showa 44). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Chiba Prefecture|Q11406137|qid=Q11406137|1=list of Shrines in Chiba Prefecture}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Makata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Makata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Narita City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Narita, Chiba]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Chiba Heritage Top 100]] [[Category:Top 500 Attractions of Bōsō Peninsula]]
